She scored many points in the basketball game.
extraExampleTranslations: Basketbol yaxshi jamoaviy ishni talab qiladi.
U basketbol o'yinida ko'p ochko to'pladi.

words.definition: Raketa va to'p bilan o'ynaladigan sport
words.definitionUz: Tennis — ikki yoki to'rt kishi o'ynaydigan tez va aniqlik talab qiluvchi sport.
words.example1: She plays tennis every weekend.
words.example1Translation: U har dam olish kunlari tennis o'ynaydi.
words.example2: Tennis is an individual sport.
words.example3: We watched a tennis match.
extraExamples: Tennis helps improve hand-eye coordination.
He won the tennis tournament last year.
extraExampleTranslations: Tennis qo'l va ko'z muvofiqlashuvini yaxshilaydi.
U o'tgan yili tennis musobaqasida g'olib bo'ldi.

words.definition: Suvda harakatlanish sporti
words.definitionUz: Suzish — suvda suzishni o'z ichiga olgan foydali jismoniy mashq.
words.example1: Swimming is good exercise.
words.example2: I go swimming twice a week.
words.example2Translation: Men haftasiga ikki marta suzishga boraman.
words.example3: She is learning swimming.
extraExamples: Swimming in the sea is very refreshing.
The swimming pool is open every day.

words.definition: Tez yurish yoki yugurish faoliyati
words.definitionUz: Yugurish — sog'liq uchun juda foydali jismoniy mashq turi.
words.example1: Running helps me stay fit.
words.example1Translation: Yugurish menga formani saqlashga yordam beradi.
words.example2: He goes running in the park.
words.example3: Running is a simple sport.
extraExamples: Morning running gives energy for the whole day.
She participates in running competitions.
extraExampleTranslations: Ertalabki yugurish kun bo'yi energiya beradi.

words.definition: Musiqa ostida harakat qilish
words.definitionUz: Raqs — musiqa bilan birga tana harakatlari orqali ifoda etish.
words.example1: She loves dancing.
words.example2: They are dancing at the party.
words.example3: Dancing is fun.
extraExamples: Traditional dancing is part of our culture.
He takes dancing lessons every week.
extraExampleTranslations: U har hafta raqs darslariga qatnaydi.

words.definition: Birgalikda ishlaydigan guruh
words.definitionUz: Jamoa — bir maqsad uchun birlashgan odamlar guruhi.
words.example1: Our team won the game.
words.example2: She is in the school team.
words.example3: Teamwork is important.
extraExamples: The team practiced every day.
Good communication helps the team succeed.

words.definition: O'yin yoki sportda ishtirok etuvchi shaxs
words.definitionUz: O'yinch — sport yoki o'yinda faol qatnashuvchi odam.
words.example1: He is a good football player.
words.example2: The player scored a goal.
words.example3: All players must follow the rules.
words.example3Translation: Barcha o'yinchilar qoidalarga rioya qilishlari kerak.
extraExamples: The best player received an award.
She is a professional tennis player.

words.definition: Qiziqarli faoliyat yoki musobaqa
words.definitionUz: O'yin — vaqtni qiziqarli o'tkazish yoki raqobat uchun qilinadigan mashg'ulot.
words.example1: We played a fun game.
words.example2: The game starts at 5 o'clock.
words.example3: Chess is a strategic game.
extraExamples: Children love playing outdoor games.
The final game was very competitive.
extraExampleTranslations: Bolalar ochiq havoda o'yin o'ynashni yaxshi ko'radilar.

words.definition: Musobaqada birinchi o'rinni egallash
words.definitionUz: G'alaba qozonmoq — raqiblardan ustun chiqib, muvaffaqiyatga erishish.
words.example1: Our team will win.
words.example2: She wants to win the prize.
words.example3: They won the match.
extraExamples: Hard work helps you win.
Who will win the competition?

words.definition: Musobaqada mag'lub bo'lish
words.definitionUz: Yutqazmoq — raqibdan pastroq natija ko'rsatish.
words.example1: Don't lose hope.
words.example2: We lost the game.
words.example3: He lost his keys.
extraExamples: It is okay to lose sometimes.
The team did not want to lose again.

words.definition: O'yin uchun yumaloq narsa
words.definitionUz: To'p — ko'p sport o'yinlarida ishlatiladigan yumaloq buyum.
words.example1: Kick the ball.
words.example2: The ball is round.
words.example3: He threw the ball.
extraExamples: The children are playing with a ball.
Basketball uses a large ball.

words.definition: Bo'sh vaqtda qilinadigan yoqtirilgan ish
words.definitionUz: Hobbi — vaqtni maroqli o'tkazish uchun qilinadigan sevimli mashg'ulot.
words.example1: My hobby is reading.
words.example2: What is your hobby?
words.example3: Painting is her hobby.
extraExamples: Gardening is a relaxing hobby.
He has many different hobbies.

words.definition: Tovushlar orqali ifodalangan san'at
words.definitionUz: Musiqa — quloqqa yoqadigan tovushlar va ritmlar.
words.example1: I listen to music.
words.example2: She plays music.
words.example3: Music makes me happy.
extraExamples: They enjoy dancing to the music.
Classical music is very beautiful.
extraExampleTranslations: Ular musiqaga qarab raqs tushishni yoqtirishadi.

words.definition: Bir joydan boshqa joyga borish
words.definitionUz: Sayohat qilmoq — yangi joylarni ko'rish va tajriba orttirish.
words.example1: I love to travel.
words.example2: They travel by plane.
words.example3: Travel broadens the mind.
extraExamples: We plan to travel to the mountains.
Traveling teaches us about different cultures.

words.definition: Quvnoq va mamnun holat
words.definitionUz: Baxtli — ichki mamnunlik va quvonch hissi.
words.example1: She is happy today.
words.example2: I feel happy.
words.example3: Happy birthday!
extraExamples: The happy children played in the park.
Making others happy is important.

words.definition: G'amgin yoki qayg'uli holat
words.definitionUz: Xafa — yo'qotish yoki yomon xabardan kelib chiqadigan his.
words.example1: He looks sad.
words.example2: The sad movie made me cry.
words.example3: Don't be sad.
extraExamples: She felt sad after losing her pet.
Sad news spread quickly.
extraExampleTranslations: Uy hayvonini yo'qotgandan keyin u xafa bo'ldi.

words.definition: Jahli chiqqan holat
words.definitionUz: G'azablangan — biror narsadan bezovta bo'lgan va g'azablangan.
words.example1: He is angry with me.
words.example2: Don't get angry.
words.example3: The angry man shouted.
extraExamples: She became angry when she heard the news.
It is better to stay calm than get angry.

words.definition: Qo'rquv hissi bilan
words.definitionUz: Qo'rqqan — xavf yoki noma'lum narsadan qo'rqish.
words.example1: I am scared of dogs.
words.example2: The child was scared.
words.example3: Don't be scared.
extraExamples: She felt scared during the storm.
He is scared to speak in public.

words.definition: Katta quvonch va intiqlik bilan
words.definitionUz: Hayajonlangan — biror yaxshi voqea oldidan quvongan holat.
words.example1: I am excited about the trip.
words.example2: The excited fans cheered.
words.example3: She looks excited.
extraExamples: The children were excited for the party.
He is excited to meet his friend.

words.definition: Qiziqish yo'q holat
words.definitionUz: Zerikkan — hech narsa qiziq bo'lmagandagi his.
words.example1: I feel bored.
words.example2: The bored students yawned.
words.example3: Don't get bored.
extraExamples: She was bored at home.
Reading helps when you are bored.

words.definition: Dam olishga muhtoj holat
words.definitionUz: Charchagan — ko'p ishlagandan keyin kuch-quvvati kamaygan.
words.example1: I am tired now.
words.example2: She feels tired after work.
words.example2Translation: U ishdan keyin charchaganini his qiladi.
words.example3: Go to bed if you are tired.
extraExamples: The tired runner sat down.
Too much work makes people tired.

words.definition: Xavotirli va bezovta holat
words.definitionUz: Asabiy — muhim voqea oldidan xavotirlanish.
words.example1: He is nervous before the exam.
words.example2: Don't be nervous.
words.example3: She felt nervous on stage.
extraExamples: The nervous student waited for results.
Deep breathing helps when you feel nervous.
extraExampleTranslations: Chuqur nafas olish asabiy bo'lganingizda yordam beradi.

words.definition: Xotirjam va osoyishta
words.definitionUz: Tinch — hech narsadan bezovta bo'lmagan holat.
words.example1: Stay calm.
words.example2: The sea is calm today.
words.example3: He spoke in a calm voice.
extraExamples: Yoga helps me stay calm.
The calm teacher helped the students.
extraExampleTranslations: Yoga menga tinch bo'lishga yordam beradi.

words.definition: Biror narsa haqida qayg'urayotgan
words.definitionUz: Xavotirli — kelajak yoki muammo haqida tashvishlanish.
words.example1: She is worried about her exam.
words.example2: Don't be worried.
words.example3: He looks worried.
extraExamples: The worried parents waited for news.
Everything will be fine, don't worry.

words.definition: Kuchli ijobiy his
words.definitionUz: Sevmoq — biror narsa yoki odamga bo'lgan chuqur mehr.
words.example1: I love my family.
words.example2: She loves music.
words.example3: Love is important.
extraExamples: They are in love.
I love traveling to new places.
extraExampleTranslations: Men yangi joylarga sayohat qilishni yaxshi ko'raman.

words.definition: Kuchli salbiy his
words.definitionUz: Nafratlanmoq — biror narsani juda yoqtirmaslik.
words.example1: I hate waiting.
words.example2: He hates spiders.
words.example3: Don't hate anyone.
extraExamples: She hates cold weather.
Hate is a strong feeling.

words.definition: Ijobiy munosabat
words.definitionUz: Yoqtirmoq — biror narsani ma'qul ko'rish.
words.example1: I like apples.
words.example2: Do you like sports?
words.example3: She likes dancing.
extraExamples: He likes reading books.
We like spending time together.

words.definition: Zavq olish
words.definitionUz: Rohatlanmoq — biror ishni yoqtirib, zavqlanish.
words.example1: I enjoy music.
words.example2: Enjoy your meal!
words.example3: They enjoy traveling.
extraExamples: She enjoys painting in her free time.
We enjoyed the party very much.
extraExampleTranslations: U bo'sh vaqtida rasm chizishdan zavqlanadi.

words.definition: Yaxshi natija bo'lishiga ishonch
words.definitionUz: Umid — kelajakda yaxshilik bo'lishiga bo'lgan ishonch.
words.example1: I hope you are well.
words.example2: There is always hope.
words.example3: She has hope for the future.
extraExamples: We hope to win the game.
Hope makes life better.
extraExampleTranslations: Biz o'yinda g'alaba qozonishga umid qilamiz.

words.definition: Og'izni tabassum bilan ochish
words.definitionUz: Tabassum qilmoq — quvonch yoki do'stlik ifodasi.
words.example1: She smiled at me.
words.example2: Smile and be happy.
words.example3: His smile is beautiful.
extraExamples: A smile can brighten someone's day.
The baby gave a big smile.

words.definition: Quvonchdan ovoz chiqarib kulish
words.definitionUz: Kulmoq — kulgili narsadan quvonib kulish.
words.example1: They laugh a lot.
words.example2: I made her laugh.
words.example3: Laughing is good for health.
extraExamples: The joke made everyone laugh.
She has a beautiful laugh.

words.definition: Ko'z yoshi to'kilishi
words.definitionUz: Yig'lamoq — qayg'u yoki kuchli hisdan ko'z yoshi chiqishi.
words.example1: The baby is crying.
words.example2: Don't cry.
words.example3: She cried after the sad movie.
extraExamples: He cried when he won the prize.
Sometimes it is okay to cry.

words.definition: Yaxshi holatda
words.definitionUz: Yaxshi — normal va qoniqarli holat.
words.example1: I am fine.
words.example2: The weather is fine.
words.example3: Everything is fine.
extraExamples: She feels fine now.
It is a fine day.
extraExampleTranslations: U hozir o'zini yaxshi his qilmoqda.

words.definition: Juda yaxshi
words.definitionUz: A'lo — yuqori darajada yaxshi.
words.example1: It is a great idea.
words.example2: You did a great job.
words.example3: The food was great.
extraExamples: We had a great time.
He is a great player.

words.definition: Katta hajmda
words.definitionUz: Katta — hajmi yoki ahamiyati katta.
words.example1: The house is big.
words.example2: He has big dreams.
words.example3: A big elephant.
extraExamples: The big city is noisy.
She bought a big cake.

words.definition: Kichik hajmda
words.definitionUz: Kichik — hajmi yoki miqdori kam.
words.example1: A small dog.
words.example2: The room is small.
words.example3: Small changes matter.
extraExamples: She lives in a small town.
The small gift made her happy.

words.definition: Katta miqdorda yoki hajmda
words.definitionUz: Katta — keng yoki katta hajmli.
words.example1: A large pizza.
words.example2: The large building.
words.example3: They have a large family.
extraExamples: The large garden has many flowers.
He ordered a large drink.

words.definition: Having a greater than average height.
words.definitionUz: O'rtacha balandlikdan yuqori bo'yga ega bo'lgan.
words.example1: My brother is very tall, so he always sits in the back row in class.
words.example1Translation: Mening akam juda baland bo'yli, shuning uchun u darsda har doim orqa qatorda o'tiradi.
words.example2: They planted tall trees along the street to provide shade.
words.example2Translation: Ular ko'chada soyani ta'minlash uchun baland daraxtlar ekishdi.
words.example3: She wore high heels to look taller for the job interview.
words.example3Translation: U ish suhbatida balandroq ko'rinish uchun baland poshnali tuflilarni kiygan edi.
extraExamples: The tall building dominated the city skyline.
Basketball players are usually quite tall.
He felt uncomfortable being the tallest person in the room.
The tall grass made it difficult to see the path.
extraExampleTranslations: Baland bino shahar siluetida ustunlik qilardi.
Basketbol o'yinchilari odatda ancha baland bo'yli bo'ladi.
Xonada eng baland odam bo'lgani uchun u o'zini noqulay his qildi.

words.definition: Having a small height or length.
words.definitionUz: Bo'yi yoki uzunligi kichik bo'lgan.
words.example1: She is quite short, so she needs a step stool to reach the top shelf.
words.example1Translation: U ancha past bo'yli, shuning uchun yuqori javonga yetish uchun taglikka muhtoj.
words.example2: The short haircut made her look younger.
words.example2Translation: Qisqa soch turmagi uni yoshroq ko'rsatdi.
words.example3: We took a short break before continuing the meeting.
words.example3Translation: Yig'ilishni davom ettirishdan oldin qisqa tanaffus oldik.
extraExamples: The short story was very touching.
He gave a short answer and left the room.
Winter days are short in this country.
She wrote a short message to her friend.
extraExampleTranslations: U qisqa javob berdi va xonadan chiqib ketdi.

words.definition: Having a great distance or duration.
words.definitionUz: Uzun masofaga yoki davomiylikka ega bo'lgan.
words.example1: The long journey tired everyone in the group.
words.example1Translation: Uzun safar guruhdagi hamma odamni charchatdi.
words.example2: She has long hair that reaches her waist.
words.example2Translation: Uning beligacha yetadigan uzun sochlari bor.
words.example3: The meeting was too long and many people got bored.
words.example3Translation: Yig'ilish juda uzun bo'ldi va ko'pchilik zerikib qoldi.
extraExamples: He wrote a long letter explaining his decision.
The long queue at the cinema disappointed us.
Long dresses are back in fashion this season.
It was a long wait before the doctor arrived.
extraExampleTranslations: U qarorini tushuntiruvchi uzun xat yozdi.
Kinoteatrdagi uzun navbat bizni xafa qildi.
Uzun ko'ylaklar bu mavsumda modaga qaytdi.

words.definition: Having a large distance from one side to the other.
words.definitionUz: Bir chetidan ikkinchi chetigacha katta masofaga ega.
words.example1: The wide road allowed cars to drive comfortably.
words.example1Translation: Keng yo'l mashinalarga qulay haydashga imkon berdi.
words.example2: He opened his eyes wide in surprise.
words.example3: The hotel room had a wide window with a beautiful view.
words.example3Translation: Mehmonxona xonasida chiroyli manzarali keng deraza bor edi.
extraExamples: The wide selection of books made her happy.
They live on a wide street near the park.
Her smile was wide and genuine.
The river is too wide to swim across.
extraExampleTranslations: Kitoblarning keng tanlovi uni xursand qildi.
Ular park yaqinidagi keng ko'chada yashaydi.
Uning tabassumi keng va samimiy edi.
Daryo suzib o'tish uchun juda keng.

words.definition: Having a lot of weight or being difficult to lift.
words.definitionUz: Ko'p vaznga ega yoki ko'tarish qiyin bo'lgan.
words.example1: The heavy box was difficult to carry upstairs.
words.example1Translation: Og'ir quti yuqoriga ko'tarish uchun qiyin edi.
words.example2: Heavy rain stopped us from going out.
words.example2Translation: Kuchli yomg'ir bizni tashqariga chiqishdan to'xtatdi.
words.example3: He has a heavy workload this month.
words.example3Translation: Bu oy uning ish yuki og'ir.
extraExamples: The heavy traffic made us late for the meeting.
She wore a heavy coat because it was cold.
The news was a heavy blow to the family.
This book is quite heavy for its size.
extraExampleTranslations: Havo sovuq bo'lgani uchun u og'ir palto kiygan edi.
Bu xabar oila uchun og'ir zarba bo'ldi.
Bu kitob o'z o'lchamiga nisbatan ancha og'ir.

words.definition: Not heavy or having little weight.
words.definitionUz: Og'ir bo'lmagan yoki kam vaznga ega.
words.example1: This suitcase is surprisingly light for its size.
words.example1Translation: Bu chamadon o'z o'lchamiga nisbatan hayratlanarli darajada engil.
words.example2: She prefers light colors for her bedroom walls.
words.example2Translation: U yotoqxona devorlari uchun ochiq ranglarni afzal ko'radi.
words.example3: We had a light lunch before the long flight.
words.example3Translation: Uzun parvozdan oldin biz engil tushlik qildik.
extraExamples: The light wind felt refreshing on a hot day.
He carried a light backpack for the hike.
The topic was light and fun to discuss.
She turned on the light to read her book.
extraExampleTranslations: Issiq kunda engil shamol tetiklantiruvchi tuyuldi.
U sayohat uchun engil ryukzak ko'tardi.
Mavzu engil va muhokama qilish uchun qiziqarli edi.
U kitob o'qish uchun chiroqni yoqdi.

words.definition: Moving or happening at high speed.
words.definitionUz: Yuqori tezlikda harakatlanadigan yoki sodir bo'ladigan.
words.example1: He is a fast runner and always wins the race.
words.example1Translation: U tez yuguruvchi va har doim poygada g'olib chiqadi.
words.example2: The fast internet connection made work much easier.
words.example2Translation: Tez internet aloqasi ishni ancha osonlashtirdi.
words.example3: She gave a fast reply to my message.
words.example3Translation: U mening xabarimga tez javob berdi.
extraExamples: The car was too fast for the narrow road.
Fast food is popular but not always healthy.
Time passes very fast when you are having fun.
He made a fast decision without thinking much.
extraExampleTranslations: Mashina tor yo'l uchun juda tez edi.
Tez ovqat mashhur, lekin har doim ham sog'lom emas.
Qiziqarli vaqt o'tkazganda vaqt juda tez o'tadi.
U ko'p o'ylamasdan tez qaror qabul qildi.

words.definition: Moving or happening at a low speed.
words.definitionUz: Past tezlikda harakatlanadigan yoki sodir bo'ladigan.
words.example1: The slow service at the restaurant disappointed the customers.
words.example1Translation: Restorandagi sekin xizmat mijozlarni xafa qildi.
words.example2: He is a slow learner but he works very hard.
words.example2Translation: U sekin o'rganuvchi, lekin juda qattiq mehnat qiladi.
words.example3: Traffic was slow due to the accident.
words.example3Translation: Baxtsiz hodisa tufayli harakat sekin edi.
extraExamples: The movie started slow but got better later.
She spoke in a slow and clear voice.
My old laptop is quite slow now.
The slow pace of life in the village is relaxing.
extraExampleTranslations: Film sekin boshlandi, lekin keyinroq yaxshilandi.
U sekin va aniq ovozda gapirdi.
Mening eski noutbukim hozir ancha sekin ishlaydi.

words.definition: Having a large distance from bottom to top.
words.definitionUz: Pastdan yuqoriga katta masofaga ega.
words.example1: The high mountain was covered with snow.
words.example1Translation: Baland tog' qor bilan qoplangan edi.
words.example2: Prices are very high in this shopping mall.
words.example2Translation: Bu savdo markazida narxlar juda baland.
words.example3: She got a high score on her English exam.
words.example3Translation: U ingliz tili imtihonida baland ball oldi.
extraExamples: The building has a high ceiling.
High temperatures are expected this week.
He has high expectations for his children.
The plane flew at a very high altitude.

words.definition: Not high or having a small distance from bottom to top.
words.definitionUz: Baland bo'lmagan yoki pastdan yuqoriga kichik masofaga ega.
words.example1: The prices are quite low during the sale season.
words.example2: She spoke in a low voice so no one could hear.
words.example2Translation: U hech kim eshitmasligi uchun past ovozda gapirdi.
words.example3: The river is low because of the dry weather.
words.example3Translation: Quruq ob-havo tufayli daryo suvi past.
extraExamples: He has a low opinion of that movie.
The low table was perfect for the children.
Temperatures will stay low until next week.
They bought the house at a low price.
extraExampleTranslations: Uning bu film haqidagi fikri past.
Past stol bolalar uchun juda mos edi.
Harorat keyingi haftagacha past bo'lib qoladi.
Ular uyni past narxda sotib olishdi.

words.definition: Having a high temperature.
words.definitionUz: Yuqori haroratga ega.
words.example1: It was too hot to go outside in the afternoon.
words.example1Translation: Tushda tashqariga chiqish uchun juda issiq edi.
words.example2: She likes drinking hot tea in the morning.
words.example2Translation: U ertalab issiq choy ichishni yaxshi ko'radi.
words.example3: The hot soup burned my tongue.
extraExamples: The weather has been hot and humid lately.
He has a hot temper and gets angry easily.
This is the hottest summer in ten years.
Be careful, the plate is still hot!
extraExampleTranslations: So'nggi paytlarda ob-havo issiq va nam bo'ldi.
Uning fe'l-atvori qizg'in va oson g'azablanadi.
Bu o'n yildagi eng issiq yoz.
Ehtiyot bo'ling, laganda hali ham issiq!

words.definition: Having a low temperature.
words.definitionUz: Past haroratga ega.
words.example1: It’s very cold outside, so wear a warm jacket.
words.example1Translation: Tashqarida juda sovuq, shuning uchun issiq kurtka kiying.
words.example2: She caught a cold after walking in the rain.
words.example3: I prefer cold drinks in summer.
words.example3Translation: Yozda men sovuq ichimliklarni afzal ko'raman.
extraExamples: The room felt cold without the heater.
His hands were cold from the freezing weather.
The news left her feeling cold and empty.
Serve the salad cold for best taste.
extraExampleTranslations: Qattiq sovuqdan uning qo'llari sovuq edi.
Bu xabar uni sovuq va bo'sh his qilishga majbur qildi.
Eng yaxshi ta'm uchun salatni sovuq holda bering.

words.definition: Having a pleasant temperature that is neither hot nor cold.
words.definitionUz: Na issiq, na sovuq, yoqimli haroratga ega.
words.example1: The warm sunshine felt nice after the rain.
words.example1Translation: Yomg'irdan keyin iliq quyosh nurlari yoqimli edi.
words.example2: She gave him a warm hug when they met.
words.example2Translation: Ular uchrashganda u unga iliq quchoq ochdi.
words.example3: Wear warm clothes because the weather is changing.
words.example3Translation: Ob-havo o'zgarayotgani uchun iliq kiyim kiying.
extraExamples: The warm welcome made us feel at home.
I like warm bread straight from the oven.
The water in the pool was pleasantly warm.
He has a warm personality and everyone likes him.
extraExampleTranslations: Iliq kutib olish bizni uyda his qilishga majbur qildi.
Men pechdan yangi chiqqan iliq nonni yaxshi ko'raman.
Uning fe'l-atvori iliq va hamma uni yaxshi ko'radi.

words.definition: Slightly cold in a pleasant way.
words.definitionUz: Yoqimli darajada biroz sovuq.
words.example1: The cool breeze felt refreshing on a hot day.
words.example1Translation: Issiq kunda salqin shabada tetiklantiruvchi edi.
words.example2: He stayed cool under pressure during the exam.
words.example2Translation: Imtihon vaqtida u bosim ostida o'zini salqin tutdi.
words.example3: Those new sneakers look really cool.
words.example3Translation: O'sha yangi krossovkalar juda zo'r ko'rinadi.
extraExamples: Keep the room cool for better sleep.
She has a cool and confident attitude.
The weather turned cool in the evening.
That was a cool trick you did with the cards!
extraExampleTranslations: Yaxshi uyqu uchun xonani salqin tuting.
Uning salqin va ishonchli munosabati bor.
Kartalar bilan qilgan o'sha hiylangiz juda ajoyib edi!

words.definition: Covered with or containing liquid.
words.definitionUz: Suv yoki suyuqlik bilan qoplangan.
words.example1: Don’t sit on the wet grass or your clothes will get dirty.
words.example1Translation: Ho'l o'tga o'tirmang, aks holda kiyimingiz iflos bo'ladi.
words.example2: The heavy rain left the streets wet and slippery.
words.example2Translation: Kuchli yomg'ir ko'chalarni ho'l va silliq qoldirdi.
words.example3: She wiped her wet hands on the towel.
words.example3Translation: U ho'l qo'llarini sochiq bilan artdi.
extraExamples: My shoes are completely wet after walking in the rain.
The wet paint sign warned people not to touch.
He came home with wet hair after swimming.
The ground is still wet from yesterday’s rain.
extraExampleTranslations: Yomg'irda yurib tuflilarim butunlay ho'l bo'ldi.
Ho'l bo'yoq yozuvi odamlarni tegmang deb ogohlantirdi.
U suzishdan keyin ho'l soch bilan uyga keldi.
Kecha yomg'irdan keyin yer hali ham ho'l.

words.definition: Not wet or without moisture.
words.definitionUz: Ho'l bo'lmagan yoki namliksiz.
words.example1: The clothes are finally dry after hanging outside.
words.example1Translation: Tashqariga osib qo'yilgandan keyin kiyimlar nihoyat quruq bo'ldi.
words.example2: The desert is a very dry place with little rain.
words.example2Translation: Cho'l juda quruq joy bo'lib, yomg'ir kam yog'adi.
words.example3: Her throat felt dry after speaking for hours.
words.example3Translation: Soatlab gapirgandan keyin uning tomog'i quruq bo'lib qoldi.
extraExamples: Please keep the matches in a dry place.
The dry weather is bad for the plants.
He gave a dry answer without any emotion.
The river became dry during the long summer.
extraExampleTranslations: U hech qanday his-tuyg'usiz quruq javob berdi.
Uzun yoz davomida daryo qurib qoldi.

words.definition: Solid and firm, not soft.
words.definitionUz: Qattiq va mustahkam, yumshoq emas.
words.example1: The bed was too hard and uncomfortable to sleep on.
words.example1Translation: To'shak juda qattiq va uxlash uchun noqulay edi.
words.example2: It was hard work but everyone felt proud at the end.
words.example2Translation: Bu qiyin ish edi, lekin oxirida hamma g'ururlandi.
words.example3: The diamond is one of the hardest materials in the world.
words.example3Translation: Olmos dunyodagi eng qattiq materiallardan biri.
extraExamples: She found the math problem very hard.
The ground was hard after weeks without rain.
He has a hard time waking up early.
Life can be hard sometimes.
extraExampleTranslations: U matematika masalasini juda qiyin deb topdi.
Bir necha hafta yomg'ir yog'magani uchun yer qattiq edi.

words.definition: Not hard or firm, easy to press.
words.definitionUz: Qattiq bo'lmagan, bosish oson.
words.example1: The soft pillow helped her sleep better.
words.example1Translation: Yumshoq yostiq unga yaxshiroq uxlashga yordam berdi.
words.example2: She spoke in a soft, gentle voice.
words.example2Translation: U yumshoq va muloyim ovozda gapirdi.
words.example3: This fabric feels very soft against the skin.
words.example3Translation: Bu mato teriga juda yumshoq tegadi.
extraExamples: The kitten has soft fur.
He has a soft heart and always helps others.
The music was soft and relaxing.
Don’t be too soft with the children.
extraExampleTranslations: Uning yuragi yumshoq va har doim boshqalarga yordam beradi.

words.definition: Producing a lot of sound.
words.definitionUz: Ko'p ovoz chiqaradigan.
words.example1: The loud music from the party could be heard from far away.
words.example1Translation: Ziyofatdan chiqayotgan baland musiqa uzoqdan eshitilardi.
words.example2: He gave a loud laugh when he heard the joke.
words.example3: The loud thunder scared the children.
extraExamples: She has a loud voice and everyone can hear her.
The neighbors complained about the loud dog.
He turned down the loud TV.
Loud noises can damage your hearing.
extraExampleTranslations: Uning ovozi baland va hamma uni eshitadi.
Qo'shnilar baland ovozli itdan shikoyat qilishdi.
Baland shovqinlar eshitishingizga zarar yetkazishi mumkin.

words.definition: Making little or no noise.
words.definitionUz: Kam yoki umuman shovqin chiqarmaydigan.
words.example1: The library is a quiet place to study.
words.example2: She is a quiet person who doesn’t talk much.
words.example3: Please be quiet during the exam.
extraExamples: The quiet street was perfect for living.
He gave a quiet answer and continued working.
The baby finally fell quiet after crying.
We enjoyed a quiet evening at home.
extraExampleTranslations: Tinch ko'cha yashash uchun juda mos edi.
U tinch javob berdi va ishini davom ettirdi.

words.definition: Free from dirt or marks.
words.definitionUz: Iflozlik yoki dog'lardan xoli.
words.example1: The hotel room was very clean and comfortable.
words.example1Translation: Mehmonxona xonasi juda toza va qulay edi.
words.example2: She keeps her house clean and organized.
words.example2Translation: U uyini toza va tartibli saqlaydi.
words.example3: Make sure your hands are clean before eating.
words.example3Translation: Ovqatdan oldin qo'llaringiz toza ekanligiga ishonch hosil qiling.
extraExamples: The air in the mountains is fresh and clean.
He changed into clean clothes after work.
The city started a clean-up campaign.
This shirt is still clean, you can wear it.
extraExampleTranslations: U ishdan keyin toza kiyimlarga o'zgardi.
Bu ko'ylak hali toza, uni kiyishingiz mumkin.

words.definition: Not clean, covered with dirt.
words.definitionUz: Toza bo'lmagan, iflozlik bilan qoplangan.
words.example1: His shoes were dirty after playing football.
words.example1Translation: Futbol o'ynagandan keyin uning tuflilari iflos bo'ldi.
words.example2: Don’t wear that dirty shirt to the meeting.
words.example3: The dirty water from the factory polluted the river.
words.example3Translation: Zavoddan chiqayotgan iflos suv daryoni ifloslantirdi.
extraExamples: The children came home with dirty hands.
The room looked dirty and messy.
She washed the dirty dishes after dinner.
Dirty streets are a problem in big cities.
extraExampleTranslations: Bolalar iflos qo'llar bilan uyga qaytishdi.
U kechki ovqatdan keyin iflos idishlarni yuvdi.

words.definition: Recently made, bought, or discovered.
words.definitionUz: Yaqinda tayyorlangan, sotib olingan yoki kashf etilgan.
words.example1: She bought a new phone last week.
words.example1Translation: U o'tgan hafta yangi telefon sotib oldi.
words.example2: The new teacher is very friendly.
words.example3: We moved into a new house in the suburbs.
words.example3Translation: Biz shahar chekkasidagi yangi uyga ko'chib o'tdik.
extraExamples: He started a new job yesterday.
The new restaurant serves excellent food.
She is learning new skills every day.
The city built a new bridge across the river.
extraExampleTranslations: U har kuni yangi ko'nikmalar o'rganmoqda.
Shahar daryo bo'ylab yangi ko'prik qurdi.

words.definition: Having lived or existed for a long time.
words.definitionUz: Uzoq vaqt yashagan yoki mavjud bo'lgan.
words.example1: My grandparents live in an old house in the village.
words.example1Translation: Bobom va buvim qishloqdagi eski uyda yashaydi.
words.example2: This is an old tradition in our family.
words.example3: The old car still works well after many years.
words.example3Translation: Eski mashina ko'p yillardan keyin ham yaxshi ishlaydi.
extraExamples: He told us an old story from his childhood.
The old man was sitting on the bench.
This building is too old and needs repair.
She collects old coins as a hobby.
extraExampleTranslations: U bolaligidan eski hikoya aytib berdi.
Bu bino juda eski va ta'mirtalab.
U sevimli mashg'uloti sifatida eski tangalarni yig'adi.

words.definition: Having lived for only a short time.
words.definitionUz: Faqat qisqa vaqt yashagan.
words.example1: The young boy helped his mother carry the bags.
words.example1Translation: Yosh bola onasiga sumkalarni ko'tarishda yordam berdi.
words.example2: She is young but very talented.
words.example3: Young people today use technology more than ever.
words.example3Translation: Bugungi yoshlar texnologiyadan har qachongidan ham ko'proq foydalanadi.
extraExamples: The young tree needs more water.
He has a young face and looks younger than his age.
The school is for young children.
She has a very young spirit.
extraExampleTranslations: Uning yuzi yosh va yoshiga nisbatan yoshroq ko'rinadi.

words.definition: Containing as much as possible.
words.definitionUz: Mumkin bo'lganicha to'la.
words.example1: The glass is full of water.
words.example2: The theater was full for the evening show.
words.example2Translation: Kechki tomosha uchun teatr to'la edi.
words.example3: I’m full after eating such a big meal.
extraExamples: Her schedule is full this week.
The bag was full of books.
The moon is full tonight.
He gave a full explanation of the problem.

words.definition: Containing nothing.
words.definitionUz: Hech narsa ichida bo'lmagan.
words.example1: The room was empty when we arrived.
words.example2: She drank the whole bottle until it was empty.
words.example3: His stomach felt empty after skipping breakfast.
words.example3Translation: Nonushtani o'tkazib yuborgani uchun qorni bo'sh his qilindi.
extraExamples: The streets were empty late at night.
He returned the empty box to the shop.
Her life felt empty after retirement.
The parking lot was almost empty.
extraExampleTranslations: Nafaqaga chiqqandan keyin uning hayoti bo'sh tuyulardi.

words.definition: Not closed or allowing access.
words.definitionUz: Yopilmagan yoki kirishga ruxsat beradigan.
words.example1: The shop is open until 10 p.m.
words.example2: Leave the window open for fresh air.
words.example2Translation: Toza havo uchun derazani ochiq qoldiring.
words.example3: She has an open mind and likes new ideas.
words.example3Translation: Uning fikri ochiq va yangi g'oyalarni yaxshi ko'radi.
extraExamples: The door was wide open.
They have an open relationship with their children.
The competition is open to everyone.
Keep your books open to page 45.

words.definition: Not open or shut.
words.definitionUz: Ochiq bo'lmagan yoki yopilgan.
words.example1: The museum is closed on Mondays.
words.example2: All the windows were closed during the storm.
words.example2Translation: Bo'ron paytida barcha derazalar yopiq edi.
words.example3: The shop is closed for renovation.
extraExamples: He kept his eyes closed while listening to music.
The case is now closed.
The road was closed due to construction.
Her mind is closed to new suggestions.
extraExampleTranslations: U musiqa tinglayotganda ko'zlarini yumib turardi.

words.definition: Correct or true.
words.definitionUz: To'g'ri yoki haqiqiy.
words.example1: She gave the right answer to the question.
words.example2: It’s not right to lie to your friends.
words.example3: Turn right at the traffic lights.
extraExamples: You have the right to remain silent.
He is the right person for this job.
Everything feels right today.
Make sure you spell the word right.
extraExampleTranslations: U bu ish uchun to'g'ri odam.
So'zni to'g'ri yozganingizga ishonch hosil qiling.

words.definition: Not correct or not true.
words.definitionUz: To'g'ri bo'lmagan yoki haqiqiy emas.
words.example1: I think you have the wrong number.
words.example2: It was wrong to take the money without asking.
words.example3: She realized she was on the wrong train.
extraExamples: The answer you gave is wrong.
He felt he had done something wrong.
The clock shows the wrong time.
It’s wrong to judge people by their appearance.
extraExampleTranslations: U biror noto'g'ri ish qilganini his qildi.
Odamlar haqida tashqi ko'rinishiga qarab hukm chiqarish noto'g'ri.

words.definition: Of high quality or positive.
words.definitionUz: Yuqori sifatli yoki ijobiy.
words.example1: She is a good student and always does her homework.
words.example1Translation: U yaxshi o'quvchi va har doim uy vazifasini bajaradi.
words.example2: The food at this restaurant is really good.
words.example3: It’s good to see you again after so long.
words.example3Translation: Uzoq vaqtdan keyin sizni yana ko'rish juda yaxshi.
extraExamples: He has a good heart.
This is a good opportunity for you.
The weather was good for a picnic.
She gave me some good advice.
extraExampleTranslations: U menga bir nechta yaxshi maslahat berdi.

words.definition: Of poor quality or negative.
words.definitionUz: Past sifatli yoki salbiy.
words.example1: The weather was bad, so we stayed inside.
words.example1Translation: Ob-havo yomon edi, shuning uchun ichkarida qoldik.
words.example2: Smoking is bad for your health.
words.example3: He felt bad after missing his friend’s birthday.
words.example3Translation: Do'stining tug'ilgan kunini o'tkazib yuborgani uchun u o'zini yomon his qildi.
extraExamples: This is a bad time to visit.
She had a bad dream last night.
The movie received bad reviews.
Eating too much sugar is bad for your teeth.
extraExampleTranslations: Bu joyga tashrif buyurish uchun yomon vaqt.
Juda ko'p shakar iste'mol qilish tishlaringiz uchun yomon.

words.definition: Not difficult or requiring little effort.
words.definitionUz: Qiyin bo'lmagan yoki kam kuch talab qiladigan.
words.example1: The test was easier than I expected.
words.example2: It’s easy to make friends if you are kind.
words.example2Translation: Agar mehribon bo'lsangiz, do'st orttirish oson.
words.example3: She chose the easy way to solve the problem.
words.example3Translation: U muammoni hal qilishning oson yo'lini tanladi.
extraExamples: Cooking this dish is quite easy.
The instructions are easy to follow.
Life is not always easy.
He made it look easy.
extraExampleTranslations: Hayot har doim ham oson emas.

words.definition: Requiring much effort or skill to do.
words.definitionUz: Bajarish uchun ko'p kuch yoki mahorat talab qiladigan.
words.example1: Learning a new language can be difficult at first.
words.example1Translation: Yangi tilni o'rganish boshida qiyin bo'lishi mumkin.
words.example2: The mountain was difficult to climb.
words.example3: She faced a difficult decision about her career.
words.example3Translation: U kasbiy faoliyati haqida qiyin qaror oldida turardi.
extraExamples: The exam was more difficult than last year.
Living alone in a big city is difficult.
He finds math very difficult.
This puzzle is quite difficult to solve.
extraExampleTranslations: U matematikani juda qiyin deb hisoblaydi.

words.definition: Of great significance or value.
words.definitionUz: Katta ahamiyatga yoki qiymatga ega.
words.example1: Education is very important for your future.
words.example2: It is important to arrive on time.
words.example3: She received an important letter from the university.
extraExamples: Family is the most important thing in life.
This meeting is very important.
He forgot an important detail.
Exercise is important for good health.
extraExampleTranslations: Jismoniy mashqlar yaxshi sog'liq uchun muhim.

words.definition: Arousing curiosity or interest.
words.definitionUz: Qiziqish uyg'otadigan.
words.example1: The book was so interesting that I couldn’t stop reading.
words.example1Translation: Kitob shunchalik qiziqarliki, o'qishni to'xtata olmadim.
words.example2: She told us an interesting story about her travels.
words.example2Translation: U sayohatlari haqida qiziqarli hikoya aytib berdi.
words.example3: The museum had many interesting exhibits.
words.example3Translation: Muzeyda ko'plab qiziqarli eksponatlar bor edi.
extraExamples: He has an interesting job.
The lecture was not very interesting.
It’s interesting to learn about different cultures.
She is an interesting person to talk to.

words.definition: Pleasing to the senses or mind.
words.definitionUz: Ko'rish yoki his qilish uchun yoqimli.
words.example1: The view from the mountain was absolutely beautiful.
words.example1Translation: Tog'dan ochiladigan manzara mutlaqo chiroyli edi.
words.example2: She looked beautiful in her wedding dress.
words.example2Translation: U to'y ko'ylagida juda chiroyli ko'rinardi.
words.example3: It was a beautiful sunny day.
extraExamples: The garden has beautiful flowers.
He has a beautiful voice.
They spent a beautiful evening together.
The painting is beautiful and expensive.

words.definition: Causing laughter or amusement.
words.definitionUz: Kulgi yoki o'yin-kulgini keltirib chiqaradigan.
words.example1: The comedian told many funny jokes.
words.example1Translation: Komediyachi ko'plab kulgili hazillar aytib berdi.
words.example2: That was the funniest movie I’ve seen this year.
words.example2Translation: Bu bu yil ko'rgan eng kulgili film edi.
words.example3: She has a funny way of explaining things.
words.example3Translation: U narsalarni tushuntirishning kulgili usuliga ega.
extraExamples: The children laughed at the funny clown.
He made a funny face to cheer her up.
It’s funny how things turned out.
She has a funny accent.
extraExampleTranslations: U uni ko'tarinkilik uchun kulgili qiyofa oldi.

words.definition: Protected from danger or harm.
words.definitionUz: Xavf yoki zararlanishdan himoyalangan.
words.example1: The children played in a safe area.
words.example2: It is safe to drink the water here.
words.example3: She felt safe with her friends around.
words.example3Translation: Do'stlari yonida u o'zini xavfsiz his qildi.
extraExamples: Keep your money in a safe place.
The car has many safe features.
We arrived home safe and sound.
Wearing a helmet keeps you safe while riding.
extraExampleTranslations: Velosiped haydashda dubulg'a kiyish sizni xavfsiz saqlaydi.

words.definition: Having a lot of work or activities to do.
words.definitionUz: Band — ko'p ish yoki mashg'ulotlar bilan band bo'lish holati.
words.example1: She is very busy with her studies.
words.example2: I am busy right now.
words.example3: The doctor is busy today.
extraExamples: The streets are busy in the morning.
He has a busy schedule.
Why are you so busy?
The shop is always busy.
She leads a busy life.
We were busy preparing dinner.
The office is busy on Mondays.
extraExampleTranslations: Biz kechki ovqat tayyorlash bilan band edik.

words.definition: Not busy or occupied; also not costing money.
words.definitionUz: Bo'sh / Erkin — band bo'lmagan yoki pul to'lamasdan foydalanish mumkin bo'lgan holat.
words.example1: Are you free this evening?
words.example2: The book is free to download.
words.example3: I feel free when I am on holiday.
extraExamples: She has free time on weekends.
Entrance is free for children.
He is free from worries.
The seat is free.
We offer free delivery.
I am free now.
Freedom is very important.
extraExampleTranslations: U dam olish kunlarida bo'sh vaqtga ega.
Biz bepul yetkazib berishni taklif qilamiz.

words.definition: Prepared and able to do something.
words.definitionUz: Tayyor — biror ishni bajarishga tayyorlangan va qodir holat.
words.example1: Dinner is ready.
words.example2: Are you ready to go?
words.example3: She is ready for the exam.
extraExamples: I am ready to help.
The students are ready for class.
Get ready for the trip.
Everything is ready.
He wasn't ready for the news.
The car is ready.
We are ready to start.

words.definition: Not the same as something else.
words.definitionUz: Boshqacha / Farqli — boshqa narsadan farq qiladigan holat.
words.example1: This book is different from that one.
words.example2: People have different opinions.
words.example3: She wears different clothes every day.
words.example3Translation: U har kuni boshqacha kiyim kiyadi.
extraExamples: The two brothers are very different.
Life in the city is different from village life.
Try something different.
We come from different countries.
Her style is different.
The weather is different today.
They have different ideas.

words.definition: Exactly like another person or thing.
words.definitionUz: Bir xil — boshqa shaxs yoki narsaga to'liq o'xshash.
words.example1: We have the same teacher.
words.example2: She looks the same as her sister.
words.example3: They wear the same clothes.
extraExamples: The two houses are the same.
We have the same opinion.
It's the same problem again.
They are the same age.
The weather is the same as yesterday.
I feel the same way.
She gave the same answer.
extraExampleTranslations: Men ham xuddi shunday his qilaman.

words.definition: Better or more important than usual things.
words.definitionUz: Maxsus — odatdagidan yaxshiroq yoki muhimroq bo'lgan narsa.
words.example1: Today is a special day.
words.example2: She has a special gift.
words.example3: This restaurant has special food.
extraExamples: He is a special friend.
We planned a special surprise.
The teacher gave special help.
This is a special occasion.
She feels special today.
The hotel has special rooms.
Make it special.
extraExampleTranslations: U bugun o'zini maxsus his qilmoqda.

words.definition: Having a lot of money or valuable things.
words.definitionUz: Boy — ko'p pul yoki qimmatbaho narsalarga ega bo'lgan holat.
words.example1: He is a rich businessman.
words.example2: The country is rich in natural resources.
words.example3: Rich people can travel a lot.
extraExamples: She comes from a rich family.
The soil is rich.
He became rich through hard work.
Rich food is not always healthy.
The library is rich in books.
They live a rich life.
The culture is very rich.
extraExampleTranslations: U qattiq mehnat orqali boyib ketdi.
Boy taom har doim ham sog'lom emas.

words.definition: Having very little money or not enough for basic needs.
words.definitionUz: Kambag'al — juda kam pulga ega yoki asosiy ehtiyojlarni qondira olmaydigan holat.
words.example1: The poor family needs help.
words.example2: He is poor but honest.
words.example3: Many poor people live in the village.
extraExamples: The soil is poor.
She feels poor in spirit.
Poor people need education.
He has poor health.
The country is poor in resources.
Don't be poor in knowledge.
They are poor but happy.
extraExampleTranslations: U ruhiy jihatdan kambag'al his qilmoqda.

words.definition: Close in distance or time.
words.definitionUz: Yaqin — masofa yoki vaqt jihatidan yaqin bo'lgan holat.
words.example1: The school is near my house.
words.example2: Summer is near.
words.example3: She lives near the park.
extraExamples: The shop is very near.
He came near to winning.
Near the end of the movie.
The station is near.
We are near the end.
Her birthday is near.
Stay near me.

words.definition: A long distance away.
words.definitionUz: Uzoq — uzoq masofada joylashgan holat.
words.example1: The city is far from here.
words.example2: She lives far away.
words.example3: It is not far to walk.
extraExamples: The journey was far.
Far from perfect.
How far is the airport?
He traveled far.
The school is not far.
Far in the future.
They came from far away.

words.definition: Friendly, generous, and considerate.
words.definitionUz: Mehribon — do'stona, saxiy va e'tiborli bo'lgan holat.
words.example1: She is a very kind person.
words.example2: Thank you for being so kind.
words.example3: He is kind to animals.
extraExamples: Be kind to others.
What a kind teacher!
Kind words can help.
She has a kind heart.
He is kind and helpful.
Kind people are loved.
Show kindness every day.

words.definition: Behaving in a kind and pleasant way.
words.definitionUz: Do'stona — mehribon va yoqimli tarzda o'zini tutish.
words.example1: The people here are very friendly.
words.example2: She gave me a friendly smile.
words.example3: It is a friendly competition.
extraExamples: He is friendly with everyone.
The hotel staff is friendly.
A friendly environment helps.
They are friendly neighbors.
She has a friendly personality.
Be friendly to new students.
The dog is friendly.

words.definition: Pleasant, kind, or enjoyable.
words.definitionUz: Yaxshi / Yoqimli — yoqimli, mehribon yoki zavqli holat.
words.example1: It is a nice day.
words.example2: She is a nice person.
words.example3: We had a nice time.
extraExamples: What a nice dress!
He is very nice to me.
The food is nice.
Have a nice day!
It was a nice surprise.
She has a nice voice.
The room is nice and clean.

words.definition: Showing good manners and respect.
words.definitionUz: Odeyli — yaxshi odob va hurmat ko'rsatish.
words.example1: He is always polite.
words.example2: It is polite to say thank you.
words.example3: The children are very polite.
extraExamples: She spoke in a polite way.
Be polite to elders.
Polite service is important.
He sent a polite letter.
The staff is polite and helpful.
Polite people are respected.
It was a polite conversation.

words.definition: Not polite; showing bad manners.
words.definitionUz: Qo'pol — o'deysiz, yomon odob ko'rsatish.
words.example1: It is rude to interrupt others.
words.example2: He was rude to the waiter.
words.example3: Don't be rude.
extraExamples: Her answer was rude.
Rude behavior is not acceptable.
He made a rude comment.
The children were rude.
It is rude to stare.
She felt bad after being rude.
Avoid rude words.
extraExampleTranslations: Qo'pollik qilgandan keyin o'zini yomon his qildi.

words.definition: Nervous or uncomfortable around other people.
words.definitionUz: Uyatchan — boshqalar oldida asabiy yoki noqulay his qilish.
words.example1: He is shy with strangers.
words.example2: The shy girl didn't speak.
words.example3: Don't be shy, come and join us.
extraExamples: She is a shy student.
He feels shy in big groups.
The cat is shy.
Overcome your shyness.
She gave a shy smile.
Shy people are often kind.
He is too shy to ask.
extraExampleTranslations: U katta guruhlarda o'zini noqulay his qiladi.

words.definition: Showing courage when facing danger or difficulty.
words.definitionUz: Jasur — xavf yoki qiyinchilik oldida jasorat ko'rsatish.
words.example1: The brave firefighter saved the child.
words.example2: She was brave during the exam.
words.example3: Be brave and try again.
words.example3Translation: Jasur bo'ling va yana sinab ko'ring.
extraExamples: He is a brave soldier.
The brave boy helped his friend.
It was a brave decision.
Brave people face their fears.
She felt brave after the speech.
The story is about a brave hero.
Be brave in difficult times.
extraExampleTranslations: Nutqdan keyin u o'zini jasur his qildi.

words.definition: Truthful and not cheating or stealing.
words.definitionUz: Halol — rostgo'y va aldash yoki o'g'irlash qilmaydigan holat.
words.example1: He is an honest man.
words.example2: It is important to be honest.
words.example3: She gave an honest answer.
extraExamples: Honest people are trusted.
He was honest about his mistake.
Honesty is the best policy.
The honest student returned the money.
She is honest with her friends.
An honest life is peaceful.
Be honest in the exam.

words.definition: Unwilling to work or use energy.
words.definitionUz: Yalqov — ishlamaslik yoki kuch sarflamaslik istagi yo'qligi.
words.example1: He is too lazy to study.
words.example2: Don't be lazy.
words.example3: The lazy cat sleeps all day.
extraExamples: Lazy students get bad results.
She felt lazy in the morning.
He has a lazy attitude.
Lazy people miss opportunities.
The weather makes me lazy.
Stop being lazy and work.
A lazy day at home.
extraExampleTranslations: U ertalab o'zini yalqov his qildi.

words.definition: Quick to understand and learn things.
words.definitionUz: Aqlli — narsalarni tez tushunadigan va o'rganadigan holat.
words.example1: She is a clever student.
words.example2: What a clever idea!
words.example3: He is clever at solving problems.
extraExamples: The clever fox escaped.
Clever people succeed.
She is clever with her hands.
He gave a clever answer.
The game is for clever children.
Be clever in your choices.
A clever plan.

words.definition: To exist or have a certain quality.
words.definitionUz: Bo'lmoq — mavjud bo'lish yoki ma'lum bir sifatga ega bo'lish.
words.example1: I want to be a doctor.
words.example2: Be careful!
words.example3: She will be happy.
extraExamples: Be kind to others.
To be or not to be.
I am happy to be here.
Be on time.
He wants to be rich.
Be yourself.
The meeting will be tomorrow.

words.definition: To possess or own something.
words.definitionUz: Ega bo'lmoq — biror narsaga egalik qilish.
words.example1: I have a new book.
words.example2: She has many friends.
words.example3: We have dinner at 7.
words.example3Translation: Biz kechki ovqatni soat 7 da yeymiz.
extraExamples: Do you have any questions?
He has a big house.
They have two children.
I have an idea.
She has blue eyes.
We have time.
Have a nice day!

words.definition: To perform an action or activity.
words.definitionUz: Qilmoq — biror harakat yoki faoliyatni bajarish.
words.example1: What do you do?
words.example2: I do my homework every day.
words.example2Translation: Men har kuni uy vazifasini bajaram.
words.example3: She does the cooking.
extraExamples: Do your best.
What did you do yesterday?
I do exercise every morning.
Do me a favor.
They do business together.
Do you like it?
He does well in school.
extraExampleTranslations: Men har kuni ertalab mashq qilaman.

words.definition: To move from one place to another.
words.definitionUz: Bormoq — bir joydan boshqa joyga harakatlanish.
words.example1: I go to school every day.
words.example2: Where did you go?
words.example3: Let's go home.
extraExamples: Go and see.
The train goes fast.
She goes to work by bus.
Time goes quickly.
We go shopping on weekends.
Go straight ahead.
He went to the doctor.
extraExampleTranslations: Biz dam olish kunlarida do'konga boramiz.

words.definition: To move towards the speaker or a place.
words.definitionUz: Kelmoq — gapiruvchi yoki biror joy tomon harakatlanish.
words.example1: Come here please.
words.example2: When will you come?
words.example3: Spring is coming.
extraExamples: Come and see me.
They came late.
Come with me.
The bus is coming.
She comes from Uzbekistan.
Come on time.
Good times are coming.

words.definition: To obtain, receive, or arrive at a place.
words.definitionUz: Olmok / Yetmoq — biror narsani olish, qabul qilish yoki joyga yetib borish.
words.example1: I get up early.
words.example2: Did you get my message?
words.example3: She got a good job.
extraExamples: Get well soon.
We get home at 6.
He got angry.
Get ready.
I get tired easily.
She gets good grades.
Let's get some food.

words.definition: To create or cause something to happen.
words.definitionUz: Qilmoq / Yasamoq — biror narsani yaratish yoki sodir bo'lishiga sabab bo'lish.
words.example1: She makes delicious food.
words.example2: I will make a cake.
words.example3: He made a mistake.
extraExamples: Make a wish.
They made a plan.
Make sure to lock the door.
She makes me happy.
He made a phone call.
We make progress.
Make yourself comfortable.

words.definition: To carry or get something; also to use time.
words.definitionUz: Olmok / Olmoq — biror narsani olib yurish yoki olish; shuningdek vaqt sarflash.
words.example1: Take this book.
words.example2: It takes time to learn.
words.example3: She takes the bus to work.
extraExamples: Take care.
I take photos.
He took the medicine.
Take a break.
She takes English lessons.
Take it easy.
We take a walk every evening.

words.definition: To hand something to someone or provide something.
words.definitionUz: Bermoq — biror narsani boshqaga topshirish yoki taqdim etish.
words.example1: Give me the book please.
words.example2: She gives good advice.
words.example3: He gave a present.
extraExamples: Give help to others.
I gave my answer.
They give money to charity.
Give it a try.
She gives her best.
Give thanks.
He gave up smoking.

words.definition: To place something in a position.
words.definitionUz: Qo'ymoq — biror narsani ma'lum bir joyga joylashtirish.
words.example1: Put the book on the table.
words.example2: She put on her coat.
words.example3: Put your ideas into words.
extraExamples: Put it here.
He put the money in his pocket.
Put on your shoes.
She put the child to bed.
Put your hand up.
We put a lot of effort.
Put the food in the fridge.

words.definition: To discover or locate something.
words.definitionUz: Topmoq — biror narsani kashf etish yoki joyini aniqlash.
words.example1: I can't find my keys.
words.example2: She found a good job.
words.example3: Find the answer in the book.
extraExamples: He found his phone.
We found a solution.
Find time to study.
She found happiness.
I found it difficult.
They found the lost dog.
Find out the truth.

words.definition: To go away from a place or stop doing something.
words.definitionUz: Ketmoq / Tark etmoq — bir joydan ketish yoki biror ishni to'xtatish.
words.example1: I leave home at 8.
words.example2: She left the room.
words.example3: Don't leave your bag.
extraExamples: He left the company.
Leave me alone.
We leave for vacation tomorrow.
She left a message.
Leave the door open.
Time to leave.
They left early.

words.definition: To carry or take something to a place.
words.definitionUz: Olib kelmoq — biror narsani bir joyga olib kelish.
words.example1: Bring your book to class.
words.example2: She brought flowers.
words.example3: Bring me some water.
extraExamples: I will bring the food.
What did you bring?
Bring your friends.
He brought good news.
Bring it back.
She brings joy to everyone.
Bring your homework.

words.definition: To let someone see something or explain something.
words.definitionUz: Ko'rsatmoq — biror narsani boshqaga ko'rsatish yoki tushuntirish.
words.example1: Show me your book.
words.example2: She showed me the way.
words.example3: The teacher showed us a video.
extraExamples: Show your ticket.
He showed courage.
Show me how to do it.
The map shows the way.
She shows kindness.
Show your work.
The film shows daily life.

words.definition: To give information to someone by speaking.
words.definitionUz: Aytmoq — gapirish orqali biror ma'lumot berish.
words.example1: Tell me the truth.
words.example2: She told a story.
words.example3: Tell him to come.
extraExamples: Tell the teacher.
I told you before.
He tells jokes.
Tell us about yourself.
Don't tell anyone.
She told the secret.
Tell me your name.

words.definition: To speak words; to express something.
words.definitionUz: Aytmoq — so'zlar aytish; biror narsani ifodalash.
words.example1: What did she say?
words.example2: Say hello to your family.
words.example3: He said yes.
extraExamples: Say it again.
She says she is tired.
Say thank you.
What do you say?
He said nothing.
Say your prayers.
I say we go now.

words.definition: To request information or something from someone.
words.definitionUz: So'ramoq — biror ma'lumot yoki narsani boshqadan iltimos qilish.
words.example1: Ask the teacher.
words.example2: She asked a question.
words.example3: Can I ask you something?
extraExamples: Ask for help.
He asked the price.
I asked my parents.
Don't be afraid to ask.
She asked for water.
Ask him to come.
They asked many questions.

words.definition: To reply to a question or request.
words.definitionUz: Javob bermoq — savol yoki iltimosga javob berish.
words.example1: Answer the question.
words.example2: She answered the phone.
words.example3: He didn't answer.
extraExamples: Answer me please.
The teacher answered all questions.
I need an answer.
She answered correctly.
Answer the door.
He answered in English.
Give a short answer.

words.definition: To have information or knowledge about something.
words.definitionUz: Bilmoq — biror narsa haqida ma'lumotga yoki bilimga ega bo'lish.
words.example1: I know the answer.
words.example2: Do you know her?
words.example3: She knows English well.
extraExamples: I don't know.
He knows many languages.
We know each other.
Know yourself.
She knows the way.
Everyone knows him.
I know how to swim.

words.definition: To have an opinion or idea in your mind.
words.definitionUz: O'ylamoq — ongda fikr yoki g'oya bo'lishi.
words.example1: I think it is good.
words.example2: What do you think?
words.example3: She is thinking about her future.
extraExamples: Think carefully.
I think so.
He thinks too much.
Think before you speak.
She thought of a solution.
We think alike.
Think positive.

words.definition: To experience a physical or emotional sensation.
words.definitionUz: Feel — jismoniy yoki ruhiy his-tuyg‘ularni boshdan kechirishni bildiradi.
words.example1: I feel tired after a long day at work.
words.example1Translation: Ishdagi uzun kundan keyin charchaganimni his qilyapman.
words.example2: She felt happy when she saw her old friends.
words.example2Translation: U eski do‘stlarini ko‘rganda o‘zini baxtli his qildi.
words.example3: The fabric feels very soft to the touch.
extraExamples: I feel excited about the upcoming trip.
He doesn't feel well today.
The room feels cold in the morning.
She felt nervous before the presentation.
I can feel the difference in quality.
They felt sorry for being late.
How do you feel about the new plan?
extraExampleTranslations: U bugun o‘zini yaxshi his qilmayapti.
U taqdimot oldidan asabiylashganini his qildi.

words.definition: To perceive with the eyes; to notice or understand something.
words.definitionUz: See — ko‘z bilan ko‘rish yoki biror narsani sezish va tushunishni bildiradi.
words.example1: I can see the mountains from my window.
words.example2: Did you see the new movie yet?
words.example3: She sees her doctor every six months.
words.example3Translation: U har olti oyda shifokoriga ko‘rinadi.
extraExamples: I see what you mean.
We saw many interesting places during our trip.
He didn't see the mistake at first.
She hopes to see her family soon.
Can you see the difference between these two?
I saw him yesterday at the market.
Let's see what happens next.
extraExampleTranslations: U yaqin orada oilasini ko‘rishni umid qilmoqda.

words.definition: To direct your eyes toward something; to appear or seem.
words.definitionUz: Look — ko‘z bilan biror narsaga qarash yoki biror holatda ko‘rinishni bildiradi.
words.example1: Look at the beautiful sunset!
words.example2: She looks tired after the long meeting.
words.example2Translation: U uzun uchrashuvdan keyin charchagan ko‘rinadi.
words.example3: He looked everywhere for his keys.
words.example3Translation: U kalitlarini hamma joyda qidirib chiqdi.
extraExamples: You look great in that new shirt.
Look both ways before crossing the street.
It looks like it's going to rain.
She looked happy to see us.
I'll look into the problem tomorrow.
He looks like his father.
Look carefully at the instructions.
extraExampleTranslations: Bu yangi ko‘ylakda juda yaxshi ko‘rinasiz.
Ko‘chadan o‘tishdan oldin ikki tomonga qarang.

words.definition: To look at something for a period of time, especially something entertaining or important.
words.definitionUz: Watch — biror narsani, ayniqsa qiziqarli yoki muhim narsani ma’lum vaqt davomida kuzatib tomosha qilishni bildiradi.
words.example1: We watch movies every Friday night.
words.example1Translation: Biz har juma oqshomida filmlar tomosha qilamiz.
words.example2: She is watching the children play in the park.
words.example3: Did you watch the football match last night?
extraExamples: I like to watch the birds in the morning.
He watches the news every evening.
Watch your step on the stairs.
They watched the plane take off.
She watches her diet carefully.
We'll watch the game together.
Watch how I do it.

words.definition: To perceive sound with the ears; to be told information.
words.definitionUz: Hear — quloq orqali tovushni eshitish yoki biror ma’lumotni bilib olishni bildiradi.
words.example1: I can hear music coming from the next room.
words.example2: Did you hear the latest news?
words.example3: She heard a strange noise outside.
extraExamples: I heard that you got a new job.
He doesn't hear very well without his hearing aid.
We heard someone calling our names.
Have you heard this song before?
She heard the good news from her sister.
I can hear the rain on the roof.
They heard about the accident on TV.

words.definition: To pay attention to sounds or what someone is saying.
words.definitionUz: Listen — tovushlarga yoki birovning gapiga diqqat qaratib tinglashni bildiradi.
words.example1: Please listen carefully to the instructions.
words.example2: She loves to listen to classical music.
words.example2Translation: U klassik musiqani tinglashni yaxshi ko‘radi.
words.example3: He never listens to advice.
extraExamples: Listen to what your teacher says.
I like listening to podcasts while driving.
They listened to the whole story.
Are you listening to me?
She listens to the radio every morning.
We listened to the birds singing.
Listen for the bell to ring.
extraExampleTranslations: Mashinada ketayotganda podkast tinglashni yaxshi ko‘raman.
U har kuni ertalab radioni tinglaydi.

words.definition: To say something using your voice; to communicate in a language.
words.definitionUz: Speak — ovoz chiqarib gapirish yoki biror tilda muloqot qilishni bildiradi.
words.example1: She speaks three languages fluently.
words.example2: He will speak at the conference tomorrow.
words.example3: Can you speak a little slower, please?
extraExamples: I speak to my parents every day.
Don't speak with your mouth full.
She spoke about her experiences.
He speaks English very well.
We need someone who can speak Spanish.
The teacher spoke to the class.
I tried to speak but no one listened.
extraExampleTranslations: U ingliz tilini juda yaxshi gapiradi.
Bizga ispan tilini biladigan odam kerak.
Gapirmoqchi bo‘ldim, lekin hech kim tinglamadi.

words.definition: To speak with someone about something; to have a conversation.
words.definitionUz: Talk — birov bilan biror mavzuda suhbatlashishni bildiradi.
words.example1: We need to talk about your plans for the future.
words.example2: They talked for hours on the phone.
words.example3: She talks too much sometimes.
words.example3Translation: U ba’zida haddan tashqari ko‘p gapiradi.
extraExamples: Let's talk about it later.
I talked to the manager yesterday.
People like to talk about the weather.
He talked about his hobbies.
We were talking when the phone rang.
Don't talk back to your parents.
She loves talking with her friends.
extraExampleTranslations: Odamlar ob-havo haqida gapirishni yaxshi ko‘rishadi.
U do‘stlari bilan suhbatlashishni yaxshi ko‘radi.

words.definition: To look at and understand written words.
words.definitionUz: Read — yozilgan so‘zlarni ko‘rib ularni tushunishni bildiradi.
words.example1: I read a book every night before sleeping.
words.example1Translation: Men har kecha uxlagandan oldin kitob o‘qiyman.
words.example2: She reads the newspaper every morning.
words.example2Translation: U har kuni ertalab gazeta o‘qiydi.
words.example3: Have you read this article yet?
extraExamples: He reads very quickly.
We read the instructions carefully.
She likes reading stories to her children.
I read an interesting post online.
They are reading the same book.
Read the question twice before answering.
She reads English books to improve her vocabulary.
extraExampleTranslations: U bolalariga hikoyalar o‘qishni yaxshi ko‘radi.
Javob berishdan oldin savolni ikki marta o‘qing.
U inglizcha kitoblarni o‘qib lug‘atini boyitmoqda.

words.definition: To put words on paper or a screen to communicate ideas.
words.definitionUz: Write — qog‘ozga yoki ekranga so‘zlar yozib fikrlarni ifodalashni bildiradi.
words.example1: I need to write an email to my boss.
words.example2: She writes in her diary every evening.
words.example2Translation: U har oqshom kundalik daftariga yozadi.
words.example3: He wrote a letter to his friend.
extraExamples: The students are writing an essay.
She writes beautiful poetry.
Please write your name here.
I wrote down the phone number.
He writes articles for a magazine.
We write messages to each other daily.
She wants to write a book one day.
extraExampleTranslations: Biz bir-birimizga har kuni xabar yozamiz.
U bir kun kitob yozishni xohlaydi.

words.definition: To gain knowledge or skill by studying or practicing.
words.definitionUz: Learn — o‘qish yoki mashq qilish orqali bilim yoki ko‘nikma egallashni bildiradi.
words.example1: I am learning how to cook Uzbek dishes.
words.example2: She learned English when she was young.
words.example3: Children learn quickly through play.
extraExamples: We learn something new every day.
He is learning to drive a car.
She learned a lot from her teacher.
You never stop learning.
I want to learn how to play the guitar.
They learned about history in school.
Learning a new language takes time.
extraExampleTranslations: Biz har kuni yangi narsa o‘rganamiz.
Yangi tilni o‘rganish vaqt talab qiladi.

words.definition: To spend time learning about a subject, especially in a detailed way.
words.definitionUz: Study — biror mavzuni chuqurroq o‘rganish uchun vaqt sarflashni bildiradi.
words.example1: She studies medicine at university.
words.example2: I need to study for tomorrow's exam.
words.example3: They study together in the library.
extraExamples: He studies hard every evening.
We are studying the effects of climate change.
She studied the map before the trip.
Students study different subjects at school.
I like to study in a quiet place.
He is studying for his final exams.
They studied the problem carefully.

words.definition: To help someone learn something by giving information or showing how to do it.
words.definitionUz: Teach — birovga ma’lumot berib yoki ko‘rsatib biror narsani o‘rgatishni bildiradi.
words.example1: She teaches English at a local school.
words.example1Translation: U mahalliy maktabda ingliz tilini o‘qitadi.
words.example2: My father taught me how to ride a bicycle.
words.example3: He teaches math to high school students.
words.example3Translation: U o‘rta maktab o‘quvchilariga matematikani o‘qitadi.
extraExamples: Experience teaches us many lessons.
She taught her children good manners.
I want to teach English online.
The coach teaches the team new strategies.
He teaches music in the evenings.
Life teaches you to be patient.
They teach computer skills at the center.

words.definition: To know the meaning of something or to grasp an idea clearly.
words.definitionUz: Understand — biror narsaning ma’nosini bilish yoki fikrni aniq tushunishni bildiradi.
words.example1: Do you understand the instructions?
words.example2: I don't understand why he is angry.
words.example3: She understands Spanish but doesn't speak it well.
words.example3Translation: U ispan tilini tushunadi, lekin yaxshi gapirmaydi.
extraExamples: He finally understood the problem.
I completely understand your situation.
It's hard to understand the rules.
She understands children very well.
We need to make sure everyone understands.
I didn't understand the joke.
He understands the importance of education.
extraExampleTranslations: Hamma tushunishiga ishonch hosil qilishimiz kerak.

words.definition: To keep something in your mind or bring it back into your mind.
words.definitionUz: Remember — biror narsani xotirada saqlash yoki esga tushirishni bildiradi.
words.example1: I can't remember where I put my keys.
words.example2: She always remembers her friends’ birthdays.
words.example2Translation: U do‘stlarining tug‘ilgan kunlarini doim eslaydi.
words.example3: Do you remember the first time we met?
extraExamples: Remember to lock the door.
He remembered everything from the meeting.
I remember playing here as a child.
She remembered to bring the documents.
We will always remember this day.
Try to remember the password.
He doesn't remember much about his childhood.
extraExampleTranslations: U uchrashuvdagi hamma narsani eslab qoldi.
U hujjatlarni olib kelishni eslab qoldi.
U bolaligi haqida ko‘p narsani eslamaydi.

words.definition: To not remember something or to fail to remember.
words.definitionUz: Forget — biror narsani eslay olmaslik yoki unutib qo‘yishni bildiradi.
words.example1: Don't forget to buy milk on your way home.
words.example1Translation: Uyga ketayotib sut sotib olishni unutmang.
words.example2: I forgot my umbrella at the office.
words.example3: She never forgets important dates.
words.example3Translation: U muhim sanalarni hech qachon unutmaydi.
extraExamples: I forgot to call my mother.
He forgot his password again.
We must not forget the lessons of history.
She forgot where she parked the car.
Don't forget your keys.
I completely forgot about the meeting.
He tries not to forget his friends’ birthdays.
extraExampleTranslations: U do‘stlarining tug‘ilgan kunlarini unutmaslikka harakat qiladi.

words.definition: To make an effort to do something; to test something.
words.definitionUz: Try — biror ishni bajarishga harakat qilish yoki sinab ko‘rishni bildiradi.
words.example1: I'll try to finish the work by tomorrow.
words.example2: She tried the new restaurant last week.
words.example2Translation: U o‘tgan hafta yangi restoranni sinab ko‘rdi.
words.example3: Try not to be late next time.
extraExamples: He is trying to learn a new language.
Try this cake, it's delicious!
We tried our best to help.
She tries to exercise every day.
Try calling him again.
I tried the new recipe.
Don't give up, keep trying.
extraExampleTranslations: Bu tortni sinab ko‘ring, juda mazali!
Biz yordam berish uchun qo‘limizdan kelganini qildik.
U har kuni sport bilan shug‘ullanishga urinadi.

words.definition: To require something because it is essential.
words.definitionUz: Need — biror narsa zarur bo‘lgani uchun unga muhtoj bo‘lishni bildiradi.
words.example1: I need some help with this project.
words.example2: She needs to rest after a long day.
words.example2Translation: U uzun kundan keyin dam olishi kerak.
words.example3: We need more time to finish the task.
words.example3Translation: Vazifani tugatish uchun ko‘proq vaqt kerak.
extraExamples: You need to eat healthy food.
He needs a new pair of shoes.
The car needs repairing.
Students need good teachers.
I need to make a phone call.
She needs to practice more.
We all need water to survive.

words.definition: To have a desire for something.
words.definitionUz: Want — biror narsani istash yoki xohlashni bildiradi.
words.example1: I want to travel around the world.
words.example2: She wants a cup of coffee.
words.example3: What do you want for dinner?
extraExamples: He wants to become a doctor.
They want to buy a new house.
I want you to be happy.
She wants to learn how to swim.
We want to help the community.
He doesn't want to go out tonight.
The children want ice cream.
extraExampleTranslations: Ular yangi uy sotib olishni xohlashadi.

words.definition: To find something enjoyable or pleasant.
words.definitionUz: Like — biror narsani yoqimli yoki zavqli deb topishni bildiradi.
words.example1: I like listening to music while working.
words.example2: She likes reading books in her free time.
words.example2Translation: U bo‘sh vaqtida kitob o‘qishni yoqtiradi.
words.example3: Do you like this new restaurant?
extraExamples: He likes playing football.
We like spending time with family.
She doesn't like waking up early.
They like traveling to new places.
I like your new haircut.
Children like eating sweets.
She likes helping others.
extraExampleTranslations: Biz oila bilan vaqt o‘tkazishni yoqtiramiz.
Ular yangi joylarga sayohat qilishni yoqtirishadi.

words.definition: To have a strong feeling of affection or deep care for someone or something.
words.definitionUz: Love — kimdir yoki biror narsaga kuchli mehr va g‘amxo‘rlik his qilishni bildiradi.
words.example1: I love spending time with my family.
words.example1Translation: Oilam bilan vaqt o‘tkazishni yaxshi ko‘raman.
words.example2: She loves chocolate ice cream.
words.example2Translation: U shokoladli muzqaymoqni juda yaxshi ko‘radi.
words.example3: He loves playing the guitar.
words.example3Translation: U gitara chalishni juda yaxshi ko‘radi.
extraExamples: They love each other very much.
We love traveling to different countries.
She loves reading mystery novels.
He loves his job.
I love the smell of fresh bread.
Children love playing outside.
She loves helping people in need.
extraExampleTranslations: Biz turli mamlakatlarga sayohat qilishni yaxshi ko‘ramiz.
U sirli romanlarni o‘qishni yaxshi ko‘radi.
U o‘z ishini juda yaxshi ko‘radi.
U muhtoj odamlarga yordam berishni yaxshi ko‘radi.

words.definition: To dislike something very strongly.
words.definitionUz: Hate — biror narsani juda kuchli yoqtirmaslikni bildiradi.
words.example1: I hate waking up early on weekends.
words.example1Translation: Dam olish kunlari erta turishni yomon ko‘raman.
words.example2: She hates rainy days.
words.example3: He hates waiting in long lines.
words.example3Translation: U uzun navbatlarda kutishni yomon ko‘radi.
extraExamples: Many people hate cold weather.
She hates doing homework.
I hate being late.
They hate wasting time.
He hates eating vegetables.
We hate traffic jams.
She hates spiders.
extraExampleTranslations: U uy vazifasini bajarishni yomon ko‘radi.
Ular vaqtni behuda sarflashni yomon ko‘rishadi.

words.definition: To get pleasure from something.
words.definitionUz: Enjoy — biror narsadan zavq va rohat olishni bildiradi.
words.example1: I enjoy walking in the park in the evening.
words.example2: She enjoys cooking for her family.
words.example2Translation: U oilasi uchun ovqat pishirishdan zavqlanadi.
words.example3: We enjoyed the movie very much.
extraExamples: He enjoys playing tennis.
They enjoy spending time together.
She enjoys reading before bed.
I enjoy learning new things.
We enjoyed our vacation a lot.
He enjoys listening to jazz.
Children enjoy playing games.
extraExampleTranslations: U uxlagandan oldin kitob o‘qishni yoqtiradi.

words.definition: To do something with a tool, method, or object for a purpose.
words.definitionUz: Use — biror asbob, usul yoki narsadan maqsadda foydalanishni bildiradi.
words.example1: I use my phone to check emails.
words.example2: She uses public transport to go to work.
words.example2Translation: U ishga borish uchun jamoat transportidan foydalanadi.
words.example3: Can I use your pen?
extraExamples: He uses a computer for his work.
We use this room for meetings.
She uses natural ingredients in her cooking.
They use English at the office.
I use this app every day.
The teacher uses different methods.
Please use the stairs.
extraExampleTranslations: Biz bu xonani uchrashuvlar uchun ishlatamiz.
U ovqat pishirishda tabiiy ingredientlardan foydalanadi.

words.definition: To make it easier for someone to do something or to assist.
words.definitionUz: Help — birovga biror ishni bajarishni osonlashtirish yoki yordam berishni bildiradi.
words.example1: Can you help me with this heavy box?
words.example1Translation: Bu og‘ir qutini ko‘tarishda yordam bera olasizmi?
words.example2: She helps her mother with the housework.
words.example2Translation: U onasiga uy ishlari bilan yordam beradi.
words.example3: The teacher helped the students understand the lesson.
words.example3Translation: O‘qituvchi o‘quvchilarga darsni tushunishda yordam berdi.
extraExamples: I need help with my homework.
He always helps his neighbors.
This medicine will help with the pain.
We helped clean the park.
She helped me choose a gift.
Technology helps us work faster.
Please help yourself to some tea.
extraExampleTranslations: U har doim qo‘shnilariga yordam beradi.
U menga sovg‘a tanlashda yordam berdi.
