words.definition: To do a job or activity to earn money or achieve something.
words.definitionUz: Work — pul topish yoki biror maqsadni amalga oshirish uchun ish bajarishni bildiradi.
words.example1: I work in a hospital as a nurse.
words.example2: He works hard every day.
words.example3: The machine isn't working properly.
extraExamples: She works from home.
We need to work together on this project.
The plan worked perfectly.
He works as a teacher.
I work out at the gym three times a week.
This medicine works well.
They work long hours.
extraExampleTranslations: Biz bu loyihada birga ishlashimiz kerak.
Haftada uch marta sport zalida mashq qilaman.

words.definition: To take part in a game or sport; to have fun.
words.definitionUz: Play — o‘yin yoki sport bilan shug‘ullanish yoki zavqlanishni bildiradi.
words.example1: The children are playing in the garden.
words.example2: He plays football every weekend.
words.example2Translation: U har dam olish kunida futbol o‘ynaydi.
words.example3: She plays the piano beautifully.
extraExamples: We played a fun game last night.
She likes to play with her dog.
The band played great music.
He plays chess very well.
Let's play outside.
They play video games together.
I play tennis with my friends.
extraExampleTranslations: U iti bilan o‘ynashni yaxshi ko‘radi.

words.definition: To move quickly on foot; to manage or operate something.
words.definitionUz: Run — oyoqda tez harakatlanish yoki biror narsani boshqarishni bildiradi.
words.example1: I run every morning to stay healthy.
words.example1Translation: Sog‘ligimni saqlash uchun har kuni ertalab yuguraman.
words.example2: She runs a small business.
words.example3: The train is running late today.
extraExamples: He ran to catch the bus.
The children ran around the playground.
This machine runs on electricity.
She runs five kilometers daily.
We need to run some tests.
The program is running smoothly.
He runs a marathon every year.
extraExampleTranslations: U avtobusga yetib olish uchun yugurdi.
U har kuni besh kilometr yuguradi.

words.definition: To move forward by putting one foot in front of the other.
words.definitionUz: Walk — bir oyog‘ingizni ikkinchisining oldiga qo‘yib oldinga harakatlanishni bildiradi.
words.example1: I walk to work every day.
words.example2: Let's walk in the park this evening.
words.example3: She walked slowly because she was tired.
extraExamples: We walked along the beach.
He walks his dog twice a day.
The baby is learning to walk.
I like walking in the rain.
They walked for hours.
She walks to school.
Walk carefully on the ice.
extraExampleTranslations: U itini kuniga ikki marta yuritadi.

words.definition: To rest your weight on your bottom, usually on a chair or the ground.
words.definitionUz: Sit — odatda stul yoki yerga dumba bilan o‘tirib dam olishni bildiradi.
words.example1: Please sit down and relax.
words.example2: The students sit at their desks during class.
words.example3: She sat quietly reading a book.
extraExamples: Let's sit outside in the sun.
He sat on the bench waiting for the bus.
We sat together at the table.
The cat sat on the windowsill.
I sit in the same seat every day.
She sat down to rest her legs.
They sat and talked for a long time.
extraExampleTranslations: Har kuni bir xil o‘rindig‘iga o‘tiramman.
U oyoqlarini dam olish uchun o‘tirdi.

words.definition: To be on your feet without moving; to rise to an upright position.
words.definitionUz: Stand — oyoqqa turib harakatsiz turish yoki tik holatga kelishni bildiradi.
words.example1: Please stand up when the teacher enters.
words.example2: I can't stand the cold weather.
words.example3: She stood at the bus stop for twenty minutes.

extraExamples: The house was built by workers.
He lives by the river.
She won by a small margin.
Pay by credit card.
Learn by heart.
The letter was sent by post.
We traveled by train.
extraExampleTranslations: U ozgina farq bilan g‘alaba qozondi.

words.definition: Towards a higher place or position.
words.definitionUz: Yuqoriroq joy yoki holatga qarab.
words.example1: Look up at the sky.
words.example2: Stand up please.
words.example3: She walked up the stairs.
extraExamples: The sun is up.
Prices went up.
Wake up early.
He ran up the hill.
Turn up the volume.
Grow up quickly.
Put your hand up.

words.definition: Towards a lower place or position.
words.definitionUz: Pastkiroq joy yoki holatga qarab.
words.example1: Sit down please.
words.example2: The ball fell down.
words.example3: She walked down the street.
extraExamples: Write it down.
The temperature went down.
Calm down.
He ran down the hill.
Put the book down.
Slow down.
Lie down and rest.

words.definition: Moving to the inside of something.
words.definitionUz: Biror narsaning ichiga harakatlanish.
words.example1: She put the keys into her bag.
words.example2: He walked into the room.
words.example3: Translate this into Uzbek.
extraExamples: Pour water into the glass.
She turned into a good student.
The car crashed into a tree.
He got into trouble.
Look into the mirror.
Divide the cake into pieces.
She is getting into music.

words.definition: In the space that separates two things.
words.definitionUz: Ikki narsa orasidagi bo‘shliqda.
words.example1: The shop is between the bank and the school.
words.example2: Divide the money between the two brothers.
words.example3: There is a good relationship between them.
extraExamples: Choose between the two options.
The meeting is between 2 and 3 pm.
A secret between friends.
The river flows between the mountains.
Read between the lines.
The age is between 20 and 30.
She sat between her parents.

words.definition: At the back of something.
words.definitionUz: Biror narsaning orqa tomonida.
words.example1: The cat is behind the door.
words.example2: He hid behind the tree.
words.example3: What is behind this decision?
extraExamples: She stood behind me.
The garden is behind the house.
He is behind in his studies.
Leave your worries behind.
The reason behind the problem.
The car behind us is fast.
Behind every success there is hard work.
extraExampleTranslations: Har bir muvaffaqiyat ortida qattiq mehnat bor.

words.definition: Very close to something on one side.
words.definitionUz: Biror narsaning bir tomonida juda yaqin.
words.example1: The bank is next to the post office.
words.example2: Sit next to me.
words.example3: She lives next to her sister.
extraExamples: The chair is next to the table.
Put it next to the window.
My friend sat next to me.
The shop is right next to the station.
She works next to her house.
The park is next to the school.
Stand next to your friend.

words.definition: Close to something in distance.
words.definitionUz: Masofa jihatidan biror narsaga yaqin.
words.example1: The school is near my house.
words.example2: She lives near the park.
words.example3: The shop is near here.
extraExamples: Near the river.
The hotel is near the airport.
Come near me.
It happened near midnight.
The station is near.
Near the end of the movie.
She is near tears.

words.definition: Below or covered by something.
words.definitionUz: Biror narsaning pastida yoki uni qoplagan holda.
words.example1: The cat is under the table.
words.example2: She hid the gift under her bed.
words.example3: The temperature is under 10 degrees.
extraExamples: Under the bridge.
Children under 18.
He works under pressure.
The book is under the pillow.
Under the sun.
The road is under repair.
She is under 30 years old.

words.definition: Above or across something.
words.definitionUz: Biror narsaning yuqorisida yoki uni kesib o‘tgan holda.
words.example1: The plane flew over the city.
words.example2: Put the blanket over the bed.
words.example3: She thinks over the problem.
extraExamples: Over the mountain.
Talk over the phone.
The game is over.
Jump over the fence.
Over 100 people.
She has power over the team.
The bridge over the river.

words.definition: Used to ask for information about something.
words.definitionUz: Biror narsa haqida ma’lumot so‘rash uchun ishlatiladi.
words.example1: What is your name?
words.example2: What do you want to eat?
words.example3: What time is it?
extraExamples: What is this?
Tell me what happened.
What color do you like?
What are you doing?
What a beautiful day!
I don't know what to say.
What is the answer?

words.definition: Used to ask about a person.
words.definitionUz: Shaxs haqida so‘rash uchun ishlatiladi.
words.example1: Who is that person?
words.example2: Who called me?
words.example3: Who is your teacher?
extraExamples: Who won the game?
I don't know who he is.
Who is coming to the party?
Tell me who you are.
Who lives here?
Who is the best player?
Who helped you?

words.definition: Used to ask about place or location.
words.definitionUz: Joy yoki manzil haqida so‘rash uchun ishlatiladi.
words.example1: Where do you live?
words.example2: Where is the bathroom?
words.example3: Where are you going?
extraExamples: Where is my phone?
I don't know where she is.
Where did you buy this?
Where are the keys?
Where is the nearest shop?
Tell me where it hurts.
Where are we?

words.definition: Used to ask about time.
words.definitionUz: Vaqt haqida so‘rash uchun ishlatiladi.
words.example1: When is your birthday?
words.example2: When did you arrive?
words.example3: When will the meeting start?
extraExamples: When are you free?
Tell me when you are ready.
When is the next train?
I remember when we met.
When does the shop open?
When it rains, we stay home.
When is the best time?

words.definition: Used to ask for a reason.
words.definitionUz: Sabab so‘rash uchun ishlatiladi.
words.example1: Why are you late?
words.example2: Why do you like this song?
words.example3: Tell me why you are sad.
extraExamples: Why not?
I don't know why.
Why is the sky blue?
Why did you do that?
Why are you smiling?
Why is it important?
Why do birds fly?

words.definition: Used to ask about the way something happens.
words.definitionUz: Biror narsa qanday sodir bo‘lishi haqida so‘rash uchun.
words.example1: How are you?
words.example2: How do you say this in Uzbek?
words.example3: How old are you?
extraExamples: How much is this?
How many people?
How did you do it?
How long does it take?
How often do you exercise?
How nice!
How to cook this?

words.definition: Used to ask about choice or to give more information.
words.definitionUz: Tanlov haqida so‘rash yoki qo‘shimcha ma’lumot berish uchun.
words.example1: Which color do you prefer?
words.example2: Which book is yours?
words.example3: This is the house which I like.
extraExamples: Which one is better?
Which way should we go?
The team which won the match.
Which friend is coming?
Which movie do you want to watch?
I don't know which to choose.
Which shirt do you like?

words.definition: Used to ask about quantity or price.
words.definitionUz: Miqdor yoki narx haqida so‘rash uchun.
words.example1: How much is this shirt?
words.example2: How much water do you drink?
words.example3: How much time do we have?
extraExamples: How much does it cost?
How much sugar do you need?
How much do you love me?
How much money do you have?
How much is the ticket?
How much does it weigh?
How much longer?

words.definition: Used to ask about number of countable things.
words.definitionUz: Sanoqli narsalarning soni haqida so‘rash uchun.
words.example1: How many students are there?
words.example2: How many apples do you want?
words.example3: How many days until the holiday?
extraExamples: How many brothers do you have?
How many books have you read?
How many people came?
How many times?
How many children?
How many kilometers?
How many questions?

words.definition: Used to ask about duration of time.
words.definitionUz: Vaqt davomiyligi haqida so‘rash uchun.
words.example1: How long will you stay?
words.example2: How long does the flight take?
words.example3: How long have you been waiting?
extraExamples: How long is the movie?
How long did it take?
How long is the river?
How long are you here?
How long does it last?
How long is the lesson?
How long have you known him?

words.definition: Used to ask about frequency.
words.definitionUz: Tez-tezlik haqida so‘rash uchun.
words.example1: How often do you exercise?
words.example2: How often does the bus come?
words.example3: How often do you visit your family?
extraExamples: How often do you eat out?
How often does it rain here?
How often do you call your mother?
How often do you travel?
How often is the meeting?
How often do you check your phone?
How often do you read books?
extraExampleTranslations: Bu yerda qanchalik tez-tez yomg‘ir yog‘adi?

words.definition: Used to ask about the time of an event.
words.definitionUz: Voqea vaqti haqida so‘rash uchun.
words.example1: What time is the meeting?
words.example2: What time do you wake up?
words.example3: What time does the train leave?
extraExamples: What time is it now?
What time does the shop open?
What time did you arrive?
What time is dinner?
What time are you free?
What time does the film start?
What time should I come?

words.definition: Used to connect words or sentences.
words.definitionUz: So‘zlar yoki jumlalarni bog‘lash uchun ishlatiladi.
words.example1: I like tea and coffee.
words.example2: She is smart and beautiful.
words.example3: He ran and jumped.
extraExamples: Bread and butter.
You and I.
Big and small.
Come and see.
Day and night.
Mum and dad.
Fast and easy.

words.definition: Used to show contrast.
words.definitionUz: Farq yoki qarama-qarshilikni bildirish uchun.
words.example1: It is expensive but good.
words.example2: I wanted to go but I was tired.
words.example3: She is young but very smart.
extraExamples: Small but strong.
I called but no one answered.
Rich but unhappy.
Try but don't give up.
Fast but safe.
Old but useful.
I like it but it's too expensive.

words.definition: Used to show choice or alternative.
words.definitionUz: Tanlov yoki alternativani bildirish uchun.
words.example1: Tea or coffee?
words.example2: Do you want apple or banana?
words.example3: You can come or stay.
extraExamples: Black or white?
Now or later?
This or that.
Win or lose.
Hot or cold?
Day or night.
Him or her.

words.definition: Used to show result or consequence.
words.definitionUz: Natija yoki oqibatni bildirish uchun.
words.example1: It is late, so I must go.
words.example1Translation: Kech bo‘ldi, shuning uchun ketishim kerak.
words.example2: She was tired, so she slept.
words.example2Translation: U charchagan edi, shuning uchun uxlab qoldi.
words.example3: It rained, so we stayed home.
words.example3Translation: Yomg‘ir yog‘di, shuning uchun uyda qoldik.
extraExamples: I am happy, so I smile.
He studied hard, so he passed.
So, what do you think?
The food is ready, so let's eat.
It is expensive, so I didn't buy it.
She is ill, so she can't come.
So many people!
extraExampleTranslations: Men baxtliman, shuning uchun tabassum qilaman.
U qattiq o‘qidi, shuning uchun o‘tdi.
Bu qimmat, shuning uchun sotib olmadim.
U kasal, shuning uchun kela olmaydi.

words.definition: Used to give a reason.
words.definitionUz: Sababni bildirish uchun.
words.example1: I am happy because I passed the exam.
words.example2: She stayed home because it rained.
words.example3: He is late because of traffic.
extraExamples: Because it is important.
I like it because it is cheap.
We canceled because of bad weather.
Because you are my friend.
She cried because she was sad.
Because of you.
I didn't go because I was busy.

words.definition: Used to talk about a possible situation.
words.definitionUz: Mumkin bo‘lgan vaziyat haqida gapirish uchun.
words.example1: If it rains, we will stay home.
words.example2: If you study, you will pass.
words.example3: Call me if you need help.
extraExamples: If I were you...
What if it is true?
If possible.
I will go if you go.
If only I knew.
Ask if you don't understand.
If it is cheap, I will buy it.
extraExampleTranslations: Agar siz borsangiz, men ham boraman.

words.definition: Used to talk about time when something happens.
words.definitionUz: Biror narsa sodir bo‘ladigan vaqt haqida gapirish uchun.
words.example1: When I am tired, I sleep.
words.example2: Call me when you arrive.
words.example3: When it is hot, we go swimming.
extraExamples: When you are ready.
When I was a child.
When the bell rings.
When it rains.
When you see her.
When the movie ends.
When I have time.

words.definition: During the time that something is happening.
words.definitionUz: Biror narsa sodir bo‘layotgan paytda.
words.example1: She sang while cooking.
words.example2: While I was sleeping, the phone rang.
words.example3: You can watch TV while eating.
extraExamples: While waiting.
While it is raining.
Read while traveling.
She smiled while talking.
While you are young.
Listen while I explain.
While on vacation.

words.definition: In addition to something.
words.definitionUz: Biror narsaga qo‘shimcha ravishda.
words.example1: I like tea. I also like coffee.
words.example1Translation: Men choyni yoqtiraman. Shuningdek, qahvani ham.
words.example2: She is smart and also kind.
words.example3: He can swim and also run fast.
words.example3Translation: U suza oladi va shuningdek tez yugura oladi.
extraExamples: Also, don't forget.
She also speaks English.
I am also tired.
The food is good and also cheap.
He also came late.
You can also call.
Also important.

words.definition: Also, or more than enough.
words.definitionUz: Shuningdek, yoki haddan tashqari.
words.example1: I am tired too.
words.example2: The coffee is too hot.
words.example3: She likes it too.
extraExamples: Me too!
Too expensive.
Too much sugar.
It is too late.
She is too young.
Too many people.
I like it too much.

words.definition: After that, or at that time.
words.definitionUz: Shundan keyin yoki o‘sha paytda.
words.example1: We ate and then slept.
words.example2: First study, then play.
words.example3: She was happy then.
extraExamples: Then what happened?
Now and then.
If not now, then when?
He left, then I left.
First this, then that.
By then it was late.
Then she smiled.
extraExampleTranslations: O‘sha paytga kelib kech bo‘lgan edi.

words.definition: Before anything else.
words.definitionUz: Boshqa hamma narsadan oldin.
words.example1: First, wash your hands.
words.example2: She came first in the race.
words.example3: This is my first time.
extraExamples: First of all.
First try, then buy.
The first lesson.
Who came first?
At first I was scared.
First place.
First things first.
extraExampleTranslations: Avval sinab ko‘ring, keyin sotib oling.

words.definition: Used as a greeting.
words.definitionUz: Salomlashish uchun ishlatiladi.
words.example1: Hello, how are you?
words.example2: She said hello to everyone.
words.example3: Hello, this is Ali speaking.
extraExamples: Hello there!
A friendly hello.
Hello from Uzbekistan.
Say hello to your family.
Hello, nice to meet you.
Hello on the phone.
A big hello.

words.definition: Informal greeting.
words.definitionUz: Norasmiy salomlashish.
words.example1: Hi, what's up?
words.example2: She waved and said hi.
words.example3: Hi everyone!
extraExamples: Hi there!
A quick hi.
Hi, I am new here.
Say hi to your brother.
Hi from me.
Friendly hi.
Hi and goodbye.

words.definition: Used when leaving someone.
words.definitionUz: Kimdandir ajralishda ishlatiladi.
words.example1: Goodbye, see you tomorrow.
words.example2: She said goodbye and left.
words.example3: Goodbye, take care.
extraExamples: Wave goodbye.
A sad goodbye.
Goodbye for now.
Say goodbye to your friends.
Goodbye and good luck.
Final goodbye.
Goodbye everyone!

words.definition: Informal way of saying goodbye.
words.definitionUz: Xayrlashishning norasmiy usuli.
words.example1: Bye, see you later.
words.example2: She waved bye.
words.example3: Bye everyone!
extraExamples: Bye for now.
A quick bye.
Bye, take care.
Say bye to your mom.
Bye bye!
See you, bye.
Bye and thanks.

words.definition: A polite word used to make a request or to accept something politely.
words.definitionUz: Iltimos — biror narsani so'rash yoki muloyimlik bilan qabul qilish uchun ishlatiladigan odobli so'z.
words.example1: Please pass me the salt.
words.example2: Can I have a glass of water, please?
words.example2Translation: Bir stakan suv olsam bo'ladimi, iltimos?
words.example3: Please be quiet in the library.
extraExamples: Please help me with this heavy box.
Thank you, please come again.
Please sit down and make yourself comfortable.
Could you speak more slowly, please?
Please don't forget to close the door.
Please let me know if you need anything.
May I please use your phone for a moment?
extraExampleTranslations: Iltimos, bu og'ir qutini ko'tarishda yordam bering.
Iltimos, o'tiring va o'zingizni qulay his qiling.
Iltimos, biror narsa kerak bo'lsa, menga xabar bering.
Telefoningizdan bir daqiqa foydalansam bo'ladimi, iltimos?

words.definition: A polite expression used to show gratitude.
words.definitionUz: Rahmat — minnatdorchilik bildirish uchun ishlatiladigan odobli ibora.
words.example1: Thank you for your help.
words.example2: Thank you very much for the gift.
words.example3: She smiled and said thank you.
extraExamples: Thank you for coming to the meeting.
Thank you, I really appreciate it.
Thank you so much for your kindness.
He forgot to say thank you.
Thank you for your patience.
Thank you in advance for your support.
A simple thank you can make someone happy.
extraExampleTranslations: Oddiy rahmat odamni baxtli qilishi mumkin.

words.definition: A word used to apologize or to express regret.
words.definitionUz: Kechirasiz yoki uzr — xato qilganingiz uchun kechirim so'rash yoki afsus bildirish uchun ishlatiladigan so'z.
words.example1: Sorry, I didn't mean to hurt you.
words.example2: Sorry for being late.
words.example3: I'm sorry about the mistake.
extraExamples: Sorry, I didn't hear what you said.
She said sorry and promised not to do it again.
Sorry to bother you so late.
I feel sorry for what happened.
Sorry, this seat is taken.
He apologized by saying sorry many times.
Sorry, I forgot your name.
extraExampleTranslations: U uzr so'radi va boshqa qilmaslikka va'da berdi.
U ko'p marta uzr so'rab kechirim so'radi.

words.definition: A polite phrase used to get someone's attention or to apologize when interrupting.
words.definitionUz: Kechirasiz — birovning e'tiborini tortish yoki suhbatga aralashganda muloyimlik bilan kechirim so'rash uchun ishlatiladigan ibora.
words.example1: Excuse me, could you tell me the time?
words.example1Translation: Kechirasiz, soat necha ekanligini ayta olasizmi?
words.example2: Excuse me, I need to pass.
words.example3: Excuse me for interrupting your conversation.
extraExamples: Excuse me, is this seat free?
Excuse me, how do I get to the station?
She said excuse me and left the room.
Excuse me for calling so late.
Excuse me, you dropped your wallet.
Excuse me while I answer this call.
He used excuse me to politely get attention.
extraExampleTranslations: U kechirasiz deb aytdi va xonadan chiqib ketdi.
U e'tiborni muloyimlik bilan tortish uchun kechirasiz dedi.

words.definition: A word used to show agreement or to answer positively.
words.definitionUz: Ha — rozi ekanligingizni yoki ijobiy javob berish uchun ishlatiladigan so'z.
words.example1: Yes, I understand.
words.example2: Would you like some tea? Yes, please.
words.example3: Yes, that is correct.
extraExamples: Yes, I will come to the party.
She said yes to the marriage proposal.
Yes, I agree with you.
Is this your book? Yes, it is.
Yes, of course I can help.
He answered yes without thinking.
Yes, the meeting is at 10 o'clock.
extraExampleTranslations: U turmushga chiqish taklifiga ha dedi.
U o'ylab o'tirmay ha deb javob berdi.

words.definition: A word used to show disagreement or to give a negative answer.
words.definitionUz: Yo'q — rozi emasligingizni yoki salbiy javob berish uchun ishlatiladigan so'z.
words.example1: No, thank you.
words.example2: Is this your car? No, it isn't.
words.example3: No, I don't like coffee.
extraExamples: No, I can't come tomorrow.
She said no to the offer.
No problem at all.
No, that is not correct.
No, I have never been there.
He shook his head and said no.
No, thanks. I'm full.
extraExampleTranslations: Yo'q, men u yerda hech qachon bo'lmaganman.

words.definition: Used to show possibility or uncertainty.
words.definitionUz: Balki — biror narsa sodir bo'lishi mumkinligini yoki noaniqlikni bildirish uchun ishlatiladi.
words.example1: Maybe we can meet tomorrow.
words.example2: Are you coming? Maybe.
words.example3: Maybe it will rain this afternoon.
words.example3Translation: Balki bugun tushdan keyin yomg'ir yog'adi.
extraExamples: Maybe I should call him.
She said maybe she would join us.
Maybe you are right.
Maybe we can try again later.
It's maybe the best solution.
Maybe next time.
Maybe I'll buy it if the price drops.
extraExampleTranslations: Agar narxi tushsa, balki sotib olaman.

words.definition: Used to show that something is obvious or to agree strongly.
words.definitionUz: Albatta — biror narsa aniq ekanligini yoki kuchli rozilik bildirish uchun ishlatiladigan ibora.
words.example1: Of course I will help you.
words.example2: Can I borrow your pen? Of course.
words.example3: Of course, it's a great idea.
extraExamples: Of course you can stay with us.
She said of course she would come.
Of course not! I would never do that.
You are tired? Of course you are after such a long day.
Of course, feel free to ask questions.
Of course I remember you.
Of course, safety comes first.
extraExampleTranslations: Albatta yo'q! Men hech qachon bunday qilmayman.
Charchadingizmi? Albatta charchagansiz, buncha uzoq kun o'tgandan keyin.

words.definition: A greeting used in the morning.
words.definitionUz: Xayrli tong — ertalab salomlashish uchun ishlatiladigan ibora.
words.example1: Good morning! How did you sleep?
words.example2: She greeted everyone with a cheerful good morning.
words.example2Translation: U hammaga quvnoq xayrli tong dedi.
words.example3: Good morning, class. Let's start the lesson.
extraExamples: Good morning, dad.
I say good morning to my neighbors every day.
Good morning! It's a beautiful day.
She sent a good morning message to her friend.
Good morning, everyone. Welcome to the meeting.
Good morning sunshine!
He replied to her good morning with a smile.
extraExampleTranslations: Men har kuni qo'shnilarimga xayrli tong deyman.
U dugonasiga xayrli tong xabarini yubordi.
Xayrli tong, hammaga. Yig'ilishga xush kelibsiz.
U uning xayrli tongiga tabassum bilan javob berdi.

words.definition: A greeting used in the afternoon.
words.definitionUz: Xayrli kun — tushdan keyin salomlashish uchun ishlatiladigan ibora.
words.example1: Good afternoon. How are you today?
words.example2: The teacher said good afternoon to the students.
words.example3: Good afternoon, sir. How can I help you?
words.example3Translation: Xayrli kun, janob. Sizga qanday yordam bera olaman?
extraExamples: Good afternoon, everyone.
She always says good afternoon politely.
Good afternoon! Nice to see you again.
Good afternoon meeting starts at 2 PM.
He greeted his colleagues with good afternoon.
Good afternoon, may I speak to the manager?
They exchanged good afternoon greetings.
extraExampleTranslations: U har doim muloyimlik bilan xayrli kun deydi.
Xayrli kun! Sizni yana ko'rishdan xursandman.
Xayrli kun yig'ilishi soat 14:00 da boshlanadi.
U hamkasblariga xayrli kun deb salomlashdi.
Xayrli kun, rahbar bilan gaplashsam bo'ladimi?
Ular bir-birlariga xayrli kun deb salomlashishdi.

words.definition: A greeting used in the evening.
words.definitionUz: Xayrli kech — kechqurun salomlashish uchun ishlatiladigan ibora.
words.example1: Good evening. Did you have a nice day?
words.example2: Good evening, ladies and gentlemen.
words.example3: She wished her family good evening.
extraExamples: Good evening, how was the traffic?
We usually say good evening after 6 PM.
Good evening, welcome to our restaurant.
He called to say good evening.
Good evening, it's nice to meet you.
She sang good evening songs at the event.
Good evening everyone, please take your seats.
extraExampleTranslations: Xayrli kech, yo'l harakati qanday edi?
Biz odatda soat 18:00 dan keyin xayrli kech deymiz.
U xayrli kech deyish uchun qo'ng'iroq qildi.
Xayrli kech, siz bilan tanishganimdan xursandman.
U tadbirda xayrli kech qo'shiqlarini kuyladi.
Xayrli kech hammaga, iltimos, o'rinlaringizga o'ting.

words.definition: A phrase used to say goodbye at night or before going to sleep.
words.definitionUz: Xayrli tun — kechasi xayrlashish yoki uxlashdan oldin aytiladigan ibora.
words.example1: Good night, sleep well.
words.example2: She kissed her children and said good night.
words.example2Translation: U bolalarini o'pib, xayrli tun dedi.
words.example3: Good night, see you tomorrow.
extraExamples: Good night, mom.
He sent her a good night message.
Good night everyone, thanks for coming.
Say good night to your grandparents.
Good night and sweet dreams.
They wished each other good night.
Good night, don't stay up too late.
extraExampleTranslations: U unga xayrli tun xabarini yubordi.
Xayrli tun hammaga, kelganingiz uchun rahmat.
Bobongiz va buvingizga xayrli tun deb ayting.

words.definition: A common greeting used to ask about someone's well-being.
words.definitionUz: Ahvolingiz qalay? — odamning hol-ahvolini so'rash uchun ishlatiladigan odatiy salomlashuv iborasi.
words.example1: Hi, how are you?
words.example2: How are you today?
words.example3: She asked her friend how are you.
words.example3Translation: U dugonasidan ahvoling qalay deb so'radi.
extraExamples: How are you feeling now?
How are you and your family?
I'm fine, how are you?
How are you doing these days?
They always ask how are you when they meet.
How are you after the long trip?
How are you? You look tired.
extraExampleTranslations: Ular uchrashganda doim ahvolingiz qalay deb so'rashadi.

words.definition: A common reply to show that you are okay.
words.definitionUz: Men yaxshiman — ahvolingizni so'raganda yaxshi ekanligingizni bildirish uchun ishlatiladigan javob.
words.example1: How are you? I'm fine, thank you.
words.example2: She said I'm fine even though she was tired.
words.example2Translation: U charchagan bo'lsa ham men yaxshiman dedi.
words.example3: I'm fine now after resting.
extraExamples: Don't worry, I'm fine.
I'm fine, just a little busy.
He replied I'm fine with a smile.
I'm fine, how about you?
She always says I'm fine even when she isn't.
I'm fine thanks to your help.
After the medicine, I'm fine.
extraExampleTranslations: U tabassum bilan men yaxshiman deb javob berdi.
U har doim yaxshi bo'lmasa ham men yaxshiman deydi.

words.definition: A polite reply to thank you.
words.definitionUz: Arzimaydi — rahmatga javoban muloyimlik bilan aytiladigan ibora.
words.example1: Thank you! You're welcome.
words.example2: You're welcome. It was my pleasure.
words.example2Translation: Arzimaydi. Bu men uchun zavq edi.
words.example3: She smiled and said you're welcome.
extraExamples: Thank you for your help. You're welcome.
You're welcome anytime.
You're welcome, glad I could help.
He always replies you're welcome politely.
You're welcome. Don't mention it.
Thank you so much! You're very welcome.
You're welcome to visit us again.
extraExampleTranslations: U har doim muloyimlik bilan arzimaydi deb javob beradi.

words.definition: Used to emphasize a high degree of something.
words.definitionUz: Juda — biror narsaning darajasini kuchaytirish uchun ishlatiladigan so'z.
words.example1: She is very happy today.
words.example2: This food is very delicious.
words.example3: He runs very fast.
extraExamples: I am very tired.
The movie was very interesting.
She speaks English very well.
It is very cold outside.
Thank you very much.
He was very surprised.
The test was very difficult.
extraExampleTranslations: U ingliz tilida juda yaxshi gapiradi.

words.definition: Used to show surprise or to emphasize truth.
words.definitionUz: Haqiqatan ham — hayratni yoki haqiqatni ta'kidlab aytish uchun ishlatiladigan so'z.
words.example1: Really? That's amazing!
words.example2: I really like this song.
words.example2Translation: Men bu qo'shiqni haqiqatan ham yaxshi ko'raman.
words.example3: She really worked hard.
words.example3Translation: U haqiqatan ham qattiq mehnat qildi.
extraExamples: Do you really mean it?
It is really hot today.
I really appreciate your help.
He really enjoyed the trip.
Really, I didn't know that.
She can really sing well.
Thank you, I really needed that.
extraExampleTranslations: U sayohatdan haqiqatan ham zavqlanib qaytdi.
U haqiqatan ham yaxshi qo'shiq aytadi.
Rahmat, menga haqiqatan ham shu kerak edi.

words.definition: Used to mean also or to a higher degree than wanted.
words.definitionUz: Ham / juda — “ham” ma'nosida yoki keragidan ortiq darajada ishlatiladi.
words.example1: I like tea too.
words.example2: This bag is too heavy.
words.example3: She speaks English too.
extraExamples: It is too late to go out.
He is too tired to work.
I want to go too.
The coffee is too sweet.
She is too young to watch this movie.
Thank you, you are too kind.
It's too much for me.
extraExampleTranslations: U bu kinoni ko'rish uchun juda yosh.

words.definition: Used to mean to a fairly high degree but not completely.
words.definitionUz: Ancha / juda — yuqori darajada, lekin to'liq emasligini bildirish uchun ishlatiladi.
words.example1: The film was quite good.
words.example2: She is quite tall for her age.
words.example2Translation: U o'z yoshiga nisbatan ancha baland.
words.example3: It is quite cold in the morning.
extraExamples: I quite like this idea.
The restaurant was quite expensive.
He is quite sure about his decision.
She speaks quite well.
It was quite a surprise.
The book is quite interesting.
We waited quite a long time.
extraExampleTranslations: U o'z qaroriga ancha ishonch hosil qilgan.

words.definition: At all times or on every occasion.
words.definitionUz: Har doim — har qanday vaqtda yoki har bir holatda sodir bo'ladigan holatni bildiradi.
words.example1: She always smiles.
words.example2: I always drink coffee in the morning.
words.example2Translation: Men har doim ertalab kofe ichaman.
words.example3: He is always late.
extraExamples: Always wear a helmet when riding a bike.
My mother always helps others.
The sun always rises in the east.
She always forgets her keys.
I will always remember this day.
He always tells the truth.
Good friends always support each other.
extraExampleTranslations: Velosiped haydaganda har doim dubulg'a kiying.
Onam har doim boshqalarga yordam beradi.
Men bu kunni har doim eslayman.
Yaxshi do'stlar har doim bir-birlarini qo'llab-quvvatlaydilar.

words.definition: In the way that happens most of the time.
words.definitionUz: Odatda — ko'pincha sodir bo'ladigan holatni bildiradi.
words.example1: I usually wake up at 7 o'clock.
words.example2: She usually goes to bed early.
words.example3: We usually eat dinner together.
words.example3Translation: Biz odatda kechki ovqatni birga yeymiz.
extraExamples: He usually walks to school.
It usually rains in spring.
She usually wears jeans.
They usually meet on weekends.
I usually read before sleeping.
The bus is usually on time.
My father usually drinks tea in the evening.
extraExampleTranslations: Ular odatda dam olish kunlarida uchrashishadi.

words.definition: Many times or frequently.
words.definitionUz: Tez-tez — ko'p marta sodir bo'ladigan holatni bildiradi.
words.example1: I often visit my grandparents.
words.example1Translation: Men bobom va buvimnikiga tez-tez boraman.
words.example2: She often forgets her phone.
words.example3: We often go to the cinema.
extraExamples: He often complains about the weather.
It often snows in winter.
She often helps her mother.
They often eat out.
I often listen to music while working.
She often wears that dress.
We often talk on the phone.
extraExampleTranslations: U tez-tez ob-havo haqida shikoyat qiladi.

words.definition: On some occasions but not always.
words.definitionUz: Ba'zan — har doim emas, lekin ba'zi paytlarda sodir bo'ladigan holatni bildiradi.
words.example1: Sometimes I walk to work.
words.example2: She sometimes feels sad.
words.example3: We sometimes eat fast food.
extraExamples: Sometimes it rains in the desert.
He sometimes forgets his homework.
I sometimes go to the gym.
Sometimes she cooks dinner.
Life is sometimes difficult.
They sometimes argue.
Sometimes the best solution is to wait.

words.definition: Not at any time.
words.definitionUz: Hech qachon — hech qanday vaqtda sodir bo'lmaydigan holatni bildiradi.
words.example1: I have never been to Paris.
words.example2: She never lies.
words.example3: He never gives up.
extraExamples: Never say never.
I never drink coffee at night.
She has never seen snow.
He never forgets important dates.
We never miss our family dinner.
Never be late for an interview.
I will never forget your kindness.
extraExampleTranslations: Men kechasi hech qachon kofe ichmayman.
U muhim sanalarni hech qachon unutmaydi.
Biz oilaviy kechki ovqatni hech qachon o'tkazib yubormaymiz.
Men sizning mehribonligingizni hech qachon unutmayman.

words.definition: Before now or before a particular time.
words.definitionUz: Allaqachon — hozirgacha yoki ma'lum vaqtgacha sodir bo'lgan holatni bildiradi.
words.example1: I have already eaten.
words.example2: She has already left.
words.example3: Have you finished your homework already?
extraExamples: The train has already departed.
I already know the answer.
She already bought the tickets.
We have already discussed this.
He is already 18 years old.
The movie has already started.
I already told you yesterday.

words.definition: Continuing up to now or at a particular time.
words.definitionUz: Hali ham — hozirgacha yoki ma'lum vaqtgacha davom etayotgan holatni bildiradi.
words.example1: She is still sleeping.
words.example2: I still don't understand.
words.example3: Are you still here?
extraExamples: It is still raining.
He still loves her.
We still have time.
She is still waiting for an answer.
I still remember that day.
The shop is still open.
They are still friends.
extraExampleTranslations: Men o'sha kunni hali ham eslayman.

words.definition: A short time ago or exactly.
words.definitionUz: Endi / faqat — yaqinda sodir bo'lgan yoki aniqlik bildirish uchun ishlatiladi.
words.example1: I just arrived.
words.example2: She just wanted to help.
words.example3: Just a moment, please.
extraExamples: He just left the office.
It is just perfect.
I just need five minutes.
She is just a child.
We just missed the bus.
Just be careful.
I just finished my work.

words.definition: At the present time.
words.definitionUz: Hozir — hozirgi vaqtda sodir bo'layotgan holatni bildiradi.
words.example1: I am busy now.
words.example2: Let's go now.
words.example3: What are you doing now?
extraExamples: Now I understand.
She lives in London now.
I need the report now.
Now is the time to act.
He is much better now.
Can you come now?
Now or never.

words.definition: In or to this place.
words.definitionUz: Shu yerda — shu joyda yoki shu joyga ma'nosini bildiradi.
words.example1: Come here, please.
words.example2: I live here.
words.example3: Put the book here.
extraExamples: Here is your coffee.
She is not here today.
Look here!
I was born here.
Here comes the bus.
Stay here and wait for me.
Here we are!
extraExampleTranslations: Shu yerda turing va meni kuting.

words.definition: In or to that place.
words.definitionUz: U yerda — u joyda yoki u joyga ma'nosini bildiradi.
words.example1: She is over there.
words.example2: Let's go there tomorrow.
words.example3: There is a park near my house.
extraExamples: I have been there before.
Put it there.
There are many people.
Look over there!
He lives there.
There is the answer.
We will meet there at 5.
extraExampleTranslations: Biz u yerda soat 5 da uchrashamiz.

words.definition: At a fast speed.
words.definitionUz: Tez — tezlik bilan bajariladigan harakatni bildiradi.
words.example1: She ran quickly to catch the bus.
words.example1Translation: U avtobusni yetib olish uchun tez yugurdi.
words.example2: Please reply quickly.
words.example3: He finished the work quickly.
extraExamples: Learn to speak English quickly.
The child ate quickly.
She changed clothes quickly.
Time passes quickly.
He walked quickly across the street.
Solve this problem quickly.
The news spread quickly.

words.definition: At a slow speed.
words.definitionUz: Sekin — past tezlikda bajariladigan harakatni bildiradi.
words.example1: Drive slowly in the rain.
words.example2: She spoke slowly so everyone could understand.
words.example2Translation: U hamma tushunishi uchun sekin gapirdi.
words.example3: The old man walked slowly.
extraExamples: Read the text slowly.
The door opened slowly.
He is learning English slowly but surely.
Time goes slowly when you are bored.
She ate her soup slowly.
The car moved slowly up the hill.
Speak more slowly, please.
extraExampleTranslations: U ingliz tilini sekin, lekin ishonch bilan o'rganmoqda.

words.definition: In a good or satisfactory way.
words.definitionUz: Yaxshi — biror ishni yaxshi bajarishni bildiradi.
words.example1: She sings very well.
words.example2: He did well in the exam.
words.example3: I know him well.
extraExamples: Everything is going well.
She speaks English well.
Eat well and stay healthy.
The team played well.
I slept well last night.
He explained it well.
She works well under pressure.

words.definition: On or to the left side.
words.definitionUz: Chap — chap tomonda yoki chap tomonga yo'nalishni bildiradi.
words.example1: Turn left at the traffic light.
words.example2: My house is on the left.
words.example3: She writes with her left hand.
extraExamples: The shop is on the left side of the street.
Take the second turning on the left.
He sat on my left.
Keep left.
The left door is locked.
She looked to the left.
Drive on the left in this country.

words.definition: On or to the right side.
words.definitionUz: O'ng — o'ng tomonda yoki o'ng tomonga yo'nalishni bildiradi.
words.example1: Turn right after the bridge.
words.example2: My right hand is stronger.
words.example3: The bank is on the right.
extraExamples: Keep right.
She sat on my right.
Take the first right.
The right answer is B.
Drive on the right in most countries.
He raised his right hand.
Look to the right before crossing.

words.definition: In a straight line or directly.
words.definitionUz: To'g'ri — to'g'ri chiziq bo'ylab yoki to'g'ridan-to'g'ri yo'nalishni bildiradi.
words.example1: Go straight ahead.
words.example2: The road goes straight for 10 km.
words.example3: Look straight into my eyes.
extraExamples: Drive straight until the next intersection.
She went straight home.
Keep going straight.
The arrow points straight up.
He walked straight past me.
Tell me straight what happened.
The picture is not hanging straight.

words.definition: Towards a higher place or position.
words.definitionUz: Yuqoriga — yuqori tomonga yoki yuqori holatga yo'nalishni bildiradi.
words.example1: Look up at the sky.
words.example2: She walked up the stairs.
words.example3: Prices are going up.
extraExamples: Stand up, please.
The sun comes up in the morning.
He climbed up the mountain.
Turn the volume up.
She grew up in a small village.
Lift your hand up.
The balloon went up into the air.

words.definition: Towards a lower place or position.
words.definitionUz: Pastga — past tomonga yoki past holatga yo'nalishni bildiradi.
words.example1: Sit down, please.
words.example2: The ball rolled down the hill.
words.example3: She looked down at the floor.
extraExamples: Write it down.
The sun goes down in the evening.
Calm down.
He fell down the stairs.
Prices are going down.
She ran down the street.
Put the book down.

words.definition: Close in distance or time.
words.definitionUz: Yaqin — masofa yoki vaqt jihatidan yaqin bo'lgan holatni bildiradi.
words.example1: The school is near my house.
words.example2: Summer is near.
words.example3: She lives near the park.
extraExamples: Come near me.
The station is quite near.
Christmas is near.
He sat near the window.
The exam is near.
There is a shop near here.
Keep near the edge.
extraExampleTranslations: Bu yerda yaqin joyda do'kon bor.

words.definition: At a long distance.
words.definitionUz: Uzoq — uzoq masofada bo'lgan holatni bildiradi.
words.example1: The airport is far from here.
words.example2: She lives far away.
words.example3: How far is the station?
extraExamples: It is not far from here.
The stars are very far.
He traveled far from home.
Don't go too far.
The shop is far from my house.
She came from far away.
The journey was long and far.
